translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And these are the names of the sons of Israel who had come into Egypt; with Jacob had they come, each with his household:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Reuben, Simeon, Levi, and Judah;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Issachar, Zebulun, and Benjamin;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Dan and Naphtali; Gad and Asher.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And all the souls that had come out of the loins of Jacob were seventy souls; and Joseph was in Egypt.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Joseph died, and all his brethren, and all that generation.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the children of Israel were fruitful, and swarmed and multiplied, and became exceeding strong; and the land was full of them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And there arose a new king over Egypt, who did not know Joseph.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to his people, Behold, the people of the children of Israel are more numerous and stronger than we.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Come on, let us deal wisely with them, lest they multiply, and it come to pass that, if war occur, they take side with our enemies and fight against us, and go up out of the land.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they set over them service-masters to oppress them with their burdens. And they built store-cities for Pharaoh, Pithom and Rameses.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But the more they afflicted them, the more they multiplied and spread; and they were distressed because of the children of Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the Egyptians made the children of Israel serve with harshness;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and they embittered their life with hard labour in clay and bricks, and in all manner of labour in the field: all their labour with which they made them serve was with harshness.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the king of Egypt spoke to the Hebrew midwives — of whom the name of the one was Shiphrah, and the name of the other was Puah —

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and he said, When ye help the Hebrew women in bearing, and see [them] on the stool, if it be a son, then ye shall kill him, but if a daughter, then she shall live.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But the midwives fearedGod, and did not as the king of Egypt had said to them, but saved the male children alive.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the king of Egypt called the midwives and said to them, Why have ye done this, and saved the male children alive?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the midwives said to Pharaoh, Because the Hebrew women are not as the Egyptian; for they are strong, and they have borne before the midwife comes to them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: AndGod dealt well with the midwives; and the people multiplied and became very strong.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass, because the midwives fearedGod, that he made them houses.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then Pharaoh charged all his people, saying, Every son that is born ye shall cast into the river, but every daughter ye shall save alive.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And a man of the house of Levi went and took a daughter of Levi.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the woman conceived, and bore a son. And she saw him that he was fair, and hid him three months.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when she could no longer hide him, she took for him an ark of reeds, and plastered it with resin and with pitch, and put the child in it, and laid [it] in the sedge on the bank of the river.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And his sister stood afar off to see what would happen to him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the daughter of Pharaoh went down to bathe in the river; and her maids went along by the river's side. And she saw the ark in the midst of the sedge, and sent her handmaid and fetched it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And she opened [it], and saw the child, and behold, the boy wept. And she had compassion on him, and said, This is [one] of the Hebrews' children.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And his sister said to Pharaoh's daughter, Shall I go and call thee a wet-nurse of the Hebrew women, that she may nurse the child for thee?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Pharaoh's daughter said to her, Go. And the damsel went and called the child's mother.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Pharaoh's daughter said to her, Take this child away and nurse it for me, and I will give [thee] thy wages. And the woman took the child and nursed it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when the child was grown, she brought him to Pharaoh's daughter, and he became her son. And she called his name Moses, and said, Because I drew him out of the water.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass in those days, when Moses was grown, that he went out to his brethren and looked on their burdens; and he saw an Egyptian smiting a Hebrew, one of his brethren.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he turned this way and that way, and when he saw that there was no man, he smote the Egyptian, and hid him in the sand.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he went out on the second day, and behold, two Hebrew men were quarrelling; and he said to him that was in the wrong, Why art thou smiting thy neighbour?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said, Who made thee ruler and judge over us? dost thou intend to kill me, as thou killedst the Egyptian? Then Moses feared, and said, Surely the matter is known.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Pharaoh heard of this matter, and sought to slay Moses. But Moses fled from before Pharaoh, and dwelt in the land of Midian. And he sat by the well.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the priest of Midian had seven daughters; and they came and drew [water], and filled the troughs, to water their father's flock.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the shepherds came and drove them away; but Moses rose and helped them, and watered their flock.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when they came to Reuel their father, he said, Why are ye come so soon to-day?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they said, An Egyptian delivered us out of the hand of the shepherds, and also drew [water] abundantly for us, and watered the flock.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to his daughters, And where is he? why then have ye left the man behind? Call him, that he may eat bread.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses consented to remain with the man; and he gave Moses Zipporah his daughter.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And she bore a son, and he called his name Gershom; for he said, I have been a sojourner in a foreign land.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass during those many days, that the king of Egypt died. And the children of Israel sighed because of the bondage, and cried; and their cry came up toGod because of the bondage;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: andGod heard their groaning, andGod remembered his covenant with Abraham, with Isaac, and with Jacob;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: andGod looked upon the children of Israel, andGod acknowledged [them].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses tended the flock of Jethro his father-in-law, the priest of Midian. And he led the flock behind the wilderness, and came to the mountain ofGod — to Horeb.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the Angel of Jehovah appeared to him in a flame of fire out of the midst of a thorn-bush: and he looked, and behold, the thorn-bush burned with fire, and the thorn-bush was not being consumed.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses said, Let me now turn aside and see this great sight, why the thorn-bush is not burnt.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah saw that he turned aside to see, andGod called to him out of the midst of the thorn-bush and said, Moses, Moses! And he said, Here am I.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said, Draw not nigh hither: loose thy sandals from off thy feet, for the place whereon thou standest is holy ground.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said, I am theGod of thy father, theGod of Abraham, theGod of Isaac, and theGod of Jacob. And Moses hid his face; for he was afraid to look atGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah said, I have seen assuredly the affliction of my people who are in Egypt, and their cry have I heard on account of their taskmasters; for I know their sorrows.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I am come down to deliver them out of the hand of the Egyptians, and to bring them up out of that land unto a good and spacious land, unto a land flowing with milk and honey, unto the place of the Canaanites, and the Hittites, and the Amorites, and the Perizzites, and the Hivites, and the Jebusites.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And now behold, the cry of the children of Israel is come unto me; and I have also seen the oppression with which the Egyptians oppress them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And now come, and I will send thee unto Pharaoh, that thou mayest bring forth my people the children of Israel out of Egypt.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses said toGod, Who am I, that I should go unto Pharaoh, and that I should bring forth the children of Israel out of Egypt?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said, For I will be with thee; and this shall be the sign to thee that I have sent thee: when thou hast brought forth the people out of Egypt, ye shall serveGod upon this mountain.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses said toGod, Behold, [when] I come unto the children of Israel, and shall say unto them, TheGod of your fathers hath sent me unto you; and they shall say, What is his name? what shall I say unto them?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: AndGod said to Moses, I AM THAT I AM. And he said, Thus shalt thou say unto the children of Israel: I AM hath sent me unto you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: AndGod said moreover to Moses, Thus shalt thou say unto the children of Israel: Jehovah, theGod of your fathers, theGod of Abraham, theGod of Isaac, and theGod of Jacob, hath sent me unto you. This is my name for ever, and this is my memorial unto all generations.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Go and gather the elders of Israel together, and say unto them, Jehovah theGod of your fathers, theGod of Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob, hath appeared to me, saying, I have indeed visited you, and [seen] that which is done unto you in Egypt;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and I have said, I will bring you up out of the affliction of Egypt, unto the land of the Canaanites, and the Hittites, and the Amorites, and the Perizzites, and the Hivites, and the Jebusites, unto a land flowing with milk and honey.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they shall hearken to thy voice. And thou shalt come, thou and the elders of Israel, unto the king of Egypt, and ye shall say unto him, Jehovah, theGod of the Hebrews, hath met with us; and now, let us go, we pray thee, three days' journey into the wilderness, that we may sacrifice to Jehovah ourGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But I know that the king of Egypt will not let you go, no, not by a powerful hand.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I will stretch out my hand and smite Egypt with all my wonders which I will do in the midst thereof; and after that he will let you go.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I will give this people favour in the eyes of the Egyptians, and it shall come to pass, when ye go out, that ye shall not go out empty;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but every woman shall ask of her neighbour, and of her that is the inmate of her house, utensils of silver, and utensils of gold, and clothing; and ye shall put [them] on your sons and on your daughters, and shall spoil the Egyptians.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses answered and said, But behold, they will not believe me, nor hearken unto my voice; for they will say, Jehovah has not appeared to thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah said to him, What is that in thy hand? And he said, A staff.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said, Cast it on the ground. And he cast it on the ground, and it became a serpent; and Moses fled from before it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah said to Moses, Stretch out thy hand and take it by the tail — and he stretched out his hand and caught it, and it became a staff in his hand —

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: that they may believe that Jehovah, theGod of their fathers, theGod of Abraham, theGod of Isaac, and theGod of Jacob, hath appeared unto thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah said moreover to him, Put now thy hand into thy bosom. And he put his hand into his bosom, and took it out, and behold, his hand was leprous, as snow.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said, Put thy hand into thy bosom again. And he put his hand into his bosom again, and took it out of his bosom, and behold, it was turned again as his flesh.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it shall come to pass, if they will not believe thee, neither hearken to the voice of the first sign, that they will believe the voice of the other sign.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it shall come to pass, if they will not believe also those two signs, neither hearken unto thy voice, that thou shalt take of the water of the river, and pour [it] on the dry [land]; and the water that thou takest out of the river shall become blood upon the dry [land].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses said to Jehovah, Ah Lord! I am not eloquent, neither heretofore nor since thou hast spoken to thy servant, for I am slow of speech and of a slow tongue.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah said to him, Who gave man a mouth? or who maketh dumb, or deaf, or seeing, or blind? [have] not I, Jehovah?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And now go, and I will be with thy mouth, and will teach thee what thou shalt say.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said, Ah Lord! send, I pray thee, by the hand [of him whom] thou wilt send.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then the anger of Jehovah was kindled against Moses, and he said, Is not Aaron the Levite thy brother? I know that he can speak well. And also behold, he goeth out to meet thee; and when he seeth thee he will be glad in his heart.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt speak unto him, and put the words in his mouth; and I will be with thy mouth, and with his mouth, and will teach you what ye shall do.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he shall speak for thee unto the people; and it shall come to pass that he shall be to thee for a mouth, and thou shalt be to him forGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt take this staff in thy hand, with which thou shalt do the signs.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses went and returned to Jethro his father-in-law, and said to him, Let me go, I pray thee, and return to my brethren who are in Egypt, that I may see whether they are yet alive. And Jethro said to Moses, Go in peace.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah said to Moses in Midian, Go, return to Egypt; for all the men are dead who sought thy life.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses took his wife and his sons, and set them riding upon an ass, and he returned to the land of Egypt. And Moses took the staff ofGod in his hand.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah said to Moses, When thou goest to return to Egypt, see that thou do all the wonders before Pharaoh that I have put in thy hand. And I will harden his heart, that he shall not let the people go.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt say to Pharaoh, Thus saith Jehovah: Israel is my son, my firstborn.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I say to thee, Let my son go, that he may serve me. And if thou refuse to let him go, behold, I will kill thy son, thy firstborn.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass on the way, in the inn, that Jehovah came upon him, and sought to slay him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then Zipporah took a stone and cut off the foreskin of her son, and cast it at his feet, and said, A bloody husband indeed art thou to me!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he let him go. Then she said, A bloody husband — because of the circumcision.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah said to Aaron, Go into the wilderness to meet Moses. And he went, and met him on the mountain ofGod, and kissed him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses told Aaron all the words of Jehovah who had sent him, and all the signs that he had commanded him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses and Aaron went and gathered all the elders of the children of Israel;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and Aaron spoke all the words that Jehovah had spoken to Moses, and did the signs before the eyes of the people.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the people believed. And when they heard that Jehovah had visited the children of Israel, and that he had seen their affliction, then they bowed their heads and worshipped.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And afterwards Moses and Aaron went in, and said to Pharaoh, Thus saith Jehovah, theGod of Israel, Let my people go that they may celebrate a feast to me in the wilderness.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Pharaoh said, Who is Jehovah, to whose voice I am to hearken to let Israel go? I do not know Jehovah, neither will I let Israel go.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they said, TheGod of the Hebrews has met with us: let us go, we pray thee, three days' journey into the wilderness, and sacrifice to Jehovah ourGod; lest he fall upon us with pestilence or with sword.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the king of Egypt said to them, Why do ye, Moses and Aaron, wish to have the people go off from their works? Away, to your burdens!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Pharaoh said, Behold the people of the land are now many, and ye wish to make them rest from their burdens.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Pharaoh commanded the same day the taskmasters of the people, and their officers, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Ye shall no more give the people straw to make brick, as heretofore: let them go and gather straw for themselves.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the number of the bricks they have made heretofore shall ye lay upon them: ye shall not diminish any of it, for they are idle; therefore they cry, saying, Let us go [and] sacrifice to ourGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Let them put heavier labour on the men, that they may be taken up with it, and not regard vain words.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the taskmasters of the people and their officers went out and spoke to the people, saying, Thus says Pharaoh: I will not give you straw:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: go ye, get yourselves straw where ye may find it; but none of your work shall be diminished.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the people were scattered abroad throughout the land of Egypt to gather stubble for straw.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the taskmasters urged [them], saying, Fulfil your labours, the daily work, as when there was straw.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the officers of the children of Israel, whom Pharaoh's taskmasters had set over them, were beaten, [and] it was said, Why have ye not fulfilled your task in making brick, both yesterday and to-day, as heretofore?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then the officers of the children of Israel came and cried to Pharaoh, saying, Why dost thou deal thus with thy bondmen?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: There is no straw given to thy bondmen, and they say to us, Make brick; and behold, thy bondmen are beaten, but it is the fault of thy people.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said, Ye are idle, idle! therefore ye say, Let us go and sacrifice to Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And now go — work! and straw shall not be given you, and ye shall deliver the measure of bricks.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the officers of the children of Israel saw [that] it stood ill with them, because it was said, Ye shall not diminish anything from your bricks, the daily work.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they met Moses and Aaron, who stood there to meet them, as they came out from Pharaoh.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they said to them, Jehovah look upon you and judge, that ye have made our odour to stink in the eyes of Pharaoh, and in the eyes of his bondmen, putting a sword into their hand to kill us!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses returned to Jehovah, and said, Lord, why hast thou done evil to this people? why now hast thou sent me?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For ever since I came to Pharaoh to speak in thy name, he hath done evil to this people; neither hast thou delivered thy people at all!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah said to Moses, Now shalt thou see what I will do to Pharaoh; for by a strong hand shall he let them go, and by a strong hand shall he drive them out of his land.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: AndGod spoke to Moses, and said to him, I am Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I appeared unto Abraham, unto Isaac, and unto Jacob, as the AlmightyGod; but by my name Jehovah I was not made known to them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I established also my covenant with them, to give them the land of Canaan, the land of their pilgrimage, in which they were sojourners.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I have heard also the groaning of the children of Israel, whom the Egyptians have forced to serve, and I have remembered my covenant.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Therefore say unto the children of Israel, I am Jehovah, and I will bring you out from under the burdens of the Egyptians, and I will deliver you from their service, and I will redeem you with a stretched-out arm, and with great judgments.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I will take you to me for a people, and will be yourGod; and ye shall know that I, Jehovah yourGod, am he who bringeth you out from under the burdens of the Egyptians.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I will bring you into the land concerning which I swore to give it unto Abraham, unto Isaac, and unto Jacob; and I will give it you for a possession: I am Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses spoke thus to the children of Israel; but they did not listen to Moses from anguish of spirit, and from hard service.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah spoke to Moses, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Go in, speak unto Pharaoh king of Egypt, that he let the children of Israel go out of his land.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses spoke before Jehovah, saying, Lo, the children of Israel do not hearken to me: how then should Pharaoh hearken to me, to me of uncircumcised lips?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah spoke to Moses and to Aaron, and gave them a commandment to the children of Israel, and to Pharaoh king of Egypt, to bring the children of Israel out of the land of Egypt.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: These are the heads of their fathers' houses: the sons of Reuben, the firstborn of Israel: Enoch and Phallu, Hezron and Carmi: these are the families of Reuben.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: — And the sons of Simeon: Jemuel, and Jamin, and Ohad, and Jachin, and Zohar, and Saul the son of a Canaanitish woman: these are the families of Simeon.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And these are the names of the sons of Levi, according to their generations: Gershon, and Kohath, and Merari. And the years of the life of Levi were a hundred and thirty-seven years.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The sons of Gershon: Libni and Shimei, according to their families.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the sons of Kohath: Amram, and Jizhar, and Hebron, and Uzziel. And the years of the life of Kohath were a hundred and thirty-three years.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the sons of Merari: Mahli and Mushi: these are the families of Levi according to their generations.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Amram took Jochebed his aunt as wife; and she bore him Aaron and Moses. And the years of the life of Amram were a hundred and thirty-seven years.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the sons of Jizhar: Korah, and Nepheg, and Zicri.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the sons of Uzziel: Mishael, and Elzaphan, and Sithri.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Aaron took Elisheba, daughter of Amminadab, sister of Nahshon, as wife; and she bore him Nadab and Abihu, Eleazar and Ithamar.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the sons of Korah: Assir, and Elkanah, and Abiasaph: these are the families of the Korahites.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Eleazar Aaron's son took [one] of the daughters of Putiel as wife; and she bore him Phinehas: these are the heads of the fathers of the Levites according to their families.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: This is that Aaron and Moses, to whom Jehovah said, Bring out the children of Israel from the land of Egypt according to their hosts.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: These are they who spoke to Pharaoh king of Egypt, to bring out the children of Israel from Egypt: this is that Moses and Aaron.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass on the day when Jehovah spoke to Moses in the land of Egypt,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: that Jehovah spoke to Moses, saying, I am Jehovah: speak thou unto Pharaoh king of Egypt all that I say unto thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses said before Jehovah, Behold, I am of uncircumcised lips, and how will Pharaoh hearken unto me?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah said to Moses, See, I have made theeGod to Pharaoh; and Aaron thy brother shall be thy prophet.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou shalt speak all that I command thee; and Aaron thy brother shall speak unto Pharaoh, that he let the children of Israel go out of his land.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I will render Pharaoh's heart obdurate, and multiply my signs and my wonders in the land of Egypt.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Pharaoh will not hearken unto you; and I will lay my hand upon Egypt, and bring forth my hosts, my people, the children of Israel, out of the land of Egypt by great judgments.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the Egyptians shall know that I am Jehovah, when I stretch forth my hand on Egypt, and bring out the children of Israel from among them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses and Aaron did as Jehovah had commanded them: so did they.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses was eighty years old, and Aaron was eighty-three years old, when they spoke to Pharaoh.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah spoke to Moses and to Aaron, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: When Pharaoh shall speak to you, saying, Do a miracle for yourselves, — then thou shalt say unto Aaron, Take thy staff and cast [it] before Pharaoh — it will become a serpent.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses and Aaron went in to Pharaoh, and did so, as Jehovah had commanded; and Aaron cast down his staff before Pharaoh, and before his bondmen, and it became a serpent.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Pharaoh also called the sages and the sorcerers; and they too, the scribes of Egypt, did so with their enchantments:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: they cast down every man his staff, and they became serpents; but Aaron's staff swallowed up their staves.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Pharaoh's heart was stubborn, and he hearkened not to them, as Jehovah had said.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah said to Moses, Pharaoh's heart is hardened: he refuseth to let the people go.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Go unto Pharaoh in the morning — behold, he will go out unto the water — and take thy stand by the bank of the river in front of him; and take in thy hand the staff that was turned into a serpent.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And say unto him, Jehovah theGod of the Hebrews has sent me to thee, saying, Let my people go, that they may serve me in the wilderness; but behold, hitherto thou hast not hearkened.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thus saith Jehovah: In this shalt thou know that I am Jehovah — behold, I will smite with the staff that is in my hand upon the water which is in the river, and it shall be turned into blood.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the fish that is in the river shall die; and the river shall stink; and the Egyptians shall loathe to drink the water out of the river.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah said to Moses, Say unto Aaron, Take thy staff, and stretch out thy hand upon the waters of the Egyptians — upon their streams, upon their rivers, and upon their ponds, and upon all their reservoirs of water, that they may become blood; and there shall be blood throughout the land of Egypt, both in [vessels of] wood and in [vessels of] stone.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses and Aaron did so, as Jehovah had commanded; and he lifted up the staff, and smote the waters that were in the river before the eyes of Pharaoh, and before the eyes of his bondmen; and all the waters that were in the river were turned into blood.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the fish that was in the river died; and the river stank, and the Egyptians could not drink the water of the river; and the blood was throughout the land of Egypt.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the scribes of Egypt did so with their sorceries; and Pharaoh's heart was stubborn, neither did he hearken to them, as Jehovah had said.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Pharaoh turned and went into his house, and took not this to heart either.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And all the Egyptians dug round about the river for water to drink; for they could not drink of the water of the river.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And seven days were fulfilled, after Jehovah had smitten the river.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah said to Moses, Go unto Pharaoh, and say unto him, Thus saith Jehovah: Let my people go, that they may serve me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And if thou refuse to let [them] go, behold, I will smite all thy borders with frogs.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the river shall swarm with frogs, and they shall go up and come into thy house, and into thy bedroom, and upon thy bed, and into the house of thy bondmen, and upon thy people, and into thine ovens, and into thy kneading-troughs.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the frogs shall come up both upon thee and upon thy people, and upon all thy bondmen.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah said to Moses, Say unto Aaron, Stretch out thy hand with thy staff over the streams, over the rivers, and over the ponds, and cause frogs to come up on the land of Egypt.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Aaron stretched out his hand over the waters of Egypt; and the frogs came up, and covered the land of Egypt.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the scribes did so with their sorceries, and brought up frogs on the land of Egypt.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Pharaoh called Moses and Aaron, and said, Intreat Jehovah, that he may take away the frogs from me and from my people; and I will let the people go, that they may sacrifice to Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses said to Pharaoh, Glory over me, for what time shall I intreat for thee, and for thy bondmen, and for thy people, to cut off the frogs from thee and from thy houses; [so that] they shall remain in the river only?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said, For to-morrow. And he said, Be it according to thy word; that thou mayest know that there is none like Jehovah ourGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the frogs shall depart from thee, and from thy houses, and from thy bondmen, and from thy people: they shall remain in the river only.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses and Aaron went out from Pharaoh; and Moses cried to Jehovah because of the frogs that he had brought against Pharaoh.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah did according to the word of Moses; and the frogs died out of the houses, out of the courts, and out of the fields.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they gathered them in heaps; and the land stank.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Pharaoh saw that there was respite; and he hardened his heart, and hearkened not to them, as Jehovah had said.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah said to Moses, Say unto Aaron, Stretch out thy staff, and smite the dust of the earth, and it shall become gnats throughout the land of Egypt.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they did so; and Aaron stretched out his hand with his staff, and smote the dust of the earth, and there arose gnats on man and on beast: all the dust of the land became gnats throughout the land of Egypt.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the scribes did so with their sorceries, to bring forth gnats; but they could not. And the gnats were on man and on beast.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then the scribes said to Pharaoh, This is the finger ofGod! But Pharaoh's heart was stubborn, and he hearkened not to them, as Jehovah had said.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah said to Moses, Rise up early in the morning, and stand before Pharaoh — behold, he will go out to the water — and say to him, Thus saith Jehovah, Let my people go, that they may serve me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For, if thou do not let my people go, behold, I will send dog-flies upon thee, and upon thy bondmen, and upon thy people, and into thy houses; and the houses of the Egyptians shall be full of dog-flies, and also the ground on which they are.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I will distinguish in that day the land of Goshen, in which my people dwell, that no dog-flies shall be there; that thou mayest know that I Jehovah am in the midst of the land.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I will put a separation between my people and thy people; to-morrow shall this sign be.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah did so; and there came dog-flies in a multitude into the house of Pharaoh, and [into] the houses of his bondmen; and throughout the land of Egypt, the land was corrupted by the dog-flies.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Pharaoh called Moses and Aaron, and said, Go, sacrifice to yourGod in the land.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses said, It is not proper to do so; for we should sacrifice the abomination of the Egyptians to Jehovah ourGod: lo, if we sacrificed the abomination of the Egyptians before their eyes, would they not stone us?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: We will go three days' journey into the wilderness, and sacrifice to Jehovah ourGod, as he shall command us.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Pharaoh said, I will let you go, that you may sacrifice to Jehovah yourGod in the wilderness; only, go not very far away: intreat for me!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses said, Behold, I go out from thee, and will intreat Jehovah; and the dog-flies will depart from Pharaoh, from his bondmen, and from his people, to-morrow; only let not Pharaoh deal deceitfully any more in not letting the people go to sacrifice to Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses went out from Pharaoh, and intreated Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah did according to the word of Moses; and he removed the dog-flies from Pharaoh, from his bondmen, and from his people; there remained not one.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Pharaoh hardened his heart this time also, and would not let the people go.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah said to Moses, Go in unto Pharaoh, and tell him, Thus saith Jehovah theGod of the Hebrews: Let my people go, that they may serve me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For if thou refuse to let them go, and shalt retain them still,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: behold, the hand of Jehovah shall be on thy cattle which is in the field, on the horses, on the asses, on the camels, on the oxen and on the sheep, with a very grievous plague.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah will distinguish between the cattle of Israel and the cattle of Egypt; and there shall nothing die of all that the children of Israel have.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah appointed a set time, saying, To-morrow will Jehovah do this thing in the land.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah did this thing on the following day, and all the cattle of Egypt died; but of the cattle of the children of Israel died not one.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Pharaoh sent, and behold, there was not one of the cattle of the Israelites dead. But the heart of Pharaoh was hardened, and he did not let the people go.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah said to Moses and to Aaron, Take to yourselves handfuls of ashes of the furnace, and let Moses scatter it toward the heavens before the eyes of Pharaoh.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it shall become fine dust over all the land of Egypt, and shall become boils on man and on cattle, breaking out [with] blisters, throughout the land of Egypt.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they took ashes of the furnace, and stood before Pharaoh; and Moses sprinkled it toward the heavens; and it became boils [with] blisters breaking out on man and on cattle.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the scribes could not stand before Moses because of the boils; for the boils were on the scribes, and on all the Egyptians.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah made Pharaoh's heart stubborn, and he did not hearken to them, as Jehovah had told Moses.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah said to Moses, Rise up early in the morning, and set thyself before Pharaoh, and say to him, Thus saith Jehovah, theGod of the Hebrews: Let my people go, that they may serve me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For I will at this time send all my plagues to thy heart, and on thy bondmen, and on thy people; that thou mayest know that there is none like me in all the earth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For now shall I put forth my hand, and I will smite thee and thy people with pestilence; and thou shalt be cut off from the earth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And for this very cause have I raised thee up, to shew thee my power; and that my name may be declared in all the earth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Dost thou still exalt thyself against my people, that thou wilt not let them go?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Behold, to-morrow about this time I will cause it to rain a very grievous hail, such as hath not been in Egypt since its foundation until now.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And now send, [and] secure thy cattle, and all that thou hast in the field: all the men and the cattle that are found in the field, and are not brought home — on them the hail shall come down, and they shall die.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He that feared the word of Jehovah among the bondmen of Pharaoh made his bondmen and his cattle flee into the houses.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But he that did not regard the word of Jehovah left his bondmen and his cattle in the field.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah said to Moses, Stretch out thy hand toward the heavens, that there may be hail throughout the land of Egypt, upon men, and upon cattle, and upon every herb of the field in the land of Egypt.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses stretched out his staff toward the heavens, and Jehovah gave thunder and hail; and the fire ran along the ground; and Jehovah rained hail on the land of Egypt.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And there was hail, and fire mingled with the hail, very grievous, such as there had been none like it in all the land of Egypt since it became a nation.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the hail smote throughout the land of Egypt all that was in the field, both men and cattle; and the hail smote every herb of the field, and broke every tree of the field.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Only in the land of Goshen, where the children of Israel were, was there no hail.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Pharaoh sent, and called Moses and Aaron, and said to them, I have sinned this time: Jehovah is the righteous [one], but I and my people are the wicked [ones].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Intreat Jehovah that it may be enough, that there be no more thunder ofGod and hail; and I will let you go, and ye shall stay no longer!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses said to him, When I go out of the city, I will spread out my hands to Jehovah: the thunder will cease, and there will be no more hail; that thou mayest know that the earth is Jehovah's.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But as to thee and thy bondmen, I know that ye do not yet fear Jehovah Elohim.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the flax and the barley were smitten; for the barley was in the ear, and the flax was bolled.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But the wheat and the spelt were not smitten; for they were not come out into ear.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses went out of the city from Pharaoh, and spread out his hands to Jehovah; and the thunders and hail ceased, and the rain was not [any more] poured on the earth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Pharaoh saw that the rain and the hail and the thunders had ceased, and he sinned yet more, and hardened his heart, he, and his bondmen.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the heart of Pharaoh was stubborn, neither would he let the children of Israel go, as Jehovah had spoken by Moses.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah said to Moses, Go in unto Pharaoh; for I have hardened his heart, and the heart of his bondmen, that I might do these my signs in their midst,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and that thou mightest tell in the ears of thy son and thy son's son what I have wrought in Egypt, and my signs which I have done among them; and ye shall know that I am Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses and Aaron came to Pharaoh, and said to him, Thus saith Jehovah theGod of the Hebrews: How long dost thou refuse to humble thyself before me? let my people go, that they may serve me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For, if thou refuse to let my people go, behold, I will to-morrow bring locusts into thy borders;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and they shall cover the face of the land, so that ye will not be able to see the land; and they shall eat the residue of that which is escaped, which ye have remaining from the hail, and shall eat every tree which ye have growing in the field;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and they shall fill thy houses, and the houses of all thy bondmen, and the houses of all the Egyptians; which neither thy fathers nor thy fathers' fathers have seen, since the day that they were upon the earth unto this day. And he turned and went out from Pharaoh.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Pharaoh's bondmen said to him, How long shall this man be a snare to us? let the men go, that they may serve Jehovah theirGod: dost thou not yet know that Egypt is ruined?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses and Aaron were brought again to Pharaoh. And he said to them, Go, serve Jehovah yourGod. Who are they that shall go?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses said, We will go with our young and with our old, with our sons and with our daughters; with our flocks and with our herds will we go; for we have a feast of Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to them, Let Jehovah be so with you, as I let you go, and your little ones: see that evil is before you!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Not so: go now, ye [that are] men, and serve Jehovah! for it is that ye have desired. And they were driven out from Pharaoh's presence.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah said to Moses, Stretch out thy hand over the land of Egypt for the locusts, that they may come up over the land of Egypt, and eat every herb of the land — all that the hail hath left.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses stretched out his staff over the land of Egypt, and Jehovah brought an east wind on the land all that day and all that night. When it was morning, the east wind brought the locusts.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the locusts went up over all the land of Egypt, and rested in all the borders of Egypt, very grievous; before them there were no such locusts as they, neither after them will be such.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they covered the face of the whole land, so that the land was darkened; and they ate every herb of the land, and all the fruit of the trees that the hail had left; and there remained not any green thing on the trees, and in the herbs of the field, throughout the land of Egypt.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Pharaoh called Moses and Aaron in haste; and he said, I have sinned against Jehovah yourGod, and against you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And now, forgive, I pray you, my sin only this time, and intreat Jehovah yourGod that he may take away from me this death only!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he went out from Pharaoh, and intreated Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah turned a very powerful west wind, which took away the locusts, and drove them into the Red Sea: there remained not one locust in all the borders of Egypt.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah made Pharaoh's heart stubborn, and he did not let the children of Israel go.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah said to Moses, Stretch out thy hand toward the heavens, that there may be darkness in the land of Egypt — so that one may feel darkness.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses stretched out his hand toward the heavens; and there was a thick darkness throughout the land of Egypt three days:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: they saw not one another, neither rose any from his place, for three days. But all the children of Israel had light in their dwellings.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Pharaoh called Moses and said, Go, serve Jehovah; only, let your flocks and your herds remain; let your little ones also go with you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses said, Thou must give also sacrifices and burnt-offerings into our hands, that we may sacrifice to Jehovah ourGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Our cattle also must go with us: there shall not a hoof be left behind; for we must take thereof to serve Jehovah ourGod; and we do not know with what we must serve Jehovah, until we come there.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Jehovah made Pharaoh's heart stubborn, and he would not let them go.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Pharaoh said to him, Get thee from me, take heed to thyself, see my face no more; for in the day thou seest my face thou shalt die.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses said, Thou hast spoken rightly: I will see thy face again no more!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah said to Moses, Yet one plague will I bring upon Pharaoh, and upon Egypt; afterwards he will let you go hence: when he shall let [you] go altogether, he shall utterly drive you out hence.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Speak now in the ears of the people, that they ask every man of his neighbour, and every woman of her neighbour, utensils of silver and utensils of gold.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah gave the people favour in the eyes of the Egyptians. Moreover the man Moses was very great in the land of Egypt — in the eyes of Pharaoh's bondmen, and in the eyes of the people.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses said, Thus saith Jehovah: About midnight I will go out into the midst of Egypt.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And all the firstborn in the land of Egypt shall die, from the firstborn of Pharaoh who sitteth on his throne, even unto the firstborn of the bondwoman that is behind the mill; and all the firstborn of cattle.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And there shall be a great cry throughout the land of Egypt, such as there hath been none like it, nor shall be like it any more.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But against any of the children of Israel shall not a dog move his tongue, against man or beast; that ye may know that Jehovah distinguisheth between the Egyptians and Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And all these thy bondmen shall come down unto me, and bow down to me, saying, Go out, thou, and all the people that follow thee; and after that I will go out. And he went out from Pharaoh in a glowing anger.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah had said to Moses, Pharaoh shall not hearken to you; that my wonders may be multiplied in the land of Egypt.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses and Aaron did all these wonders before Pharaoh; but Jehovah made Pharaoh's heart stubborn, and he did not let the children of Israel go out of his land.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah spoke to Moses and Aaron in the land of Egypt, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: This month shall be unto you the beginning of months: it shall be the first month of the year to you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Speak unto all the assembly of Israel, saying, On the tenth of this month let them take themselves each a lamb, for a father's house, a lamb for a house.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And if the household be too small for a lamb, let him and his neighbour next unto his house take [it] according to the number of the souls; each according to [the measure] of his eating shall ye count for the lamb.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Your lamb shall be without blemish, a yearling male; ye shall take [it] from the sheep, or from the goats.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And ye shall keep it until the fourteenth day of this month; and the whole congregation of the assembly of Israel shall kill it between the two evenings.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they shall take of the blood, and put [it] on the two door-posts and on the lintel of the houses in which they eat it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they shall eat the flesh in that night, roast with fire, and unleavened bread; with bitter [herbs] shall they eat it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Ye shall eat none of it raw, nor boiled at all with water, but roast with fire; its head with its legs and with its in-wards.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And ye shall let none of it remain until the morning; and what remaineth of it until the morning ye shall burn with fire.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thus shall ye eat it: your loins shall be girded, your sandals on your feet, and your staff in your hand; and ye shall eat it in haste; it is Jehovah's passover.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I will go through the land of Egypt in that night, and smite all the firstborn in the land of Egypt, both man and beast; and against all thegods of Egypt I will execute judgment: I am Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the blood shall be for you as a sign on the houses in which ye are; and when I see the blood, I will pass over you; and the plague shall not be among you for destruction, when I smite the land of Egypt.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And this day shall be unto you for a memorial; and ye shall celebrate it [as] a feast to Jehovah; throughout your generations [as] an ordinance for ever shall ye celebrate it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Seven days shall ye eat unleavened bread: on the very first day ye shall put away leaven out of your houses; for whoever eateth leavened bread from the first day until the seventh day — that soul shall be cut off from Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And on the first day ye shall have a holy convocation, and on the seventh day a holy convocation: no manner of work shall be done on them, save what is eaten by every person — that only shall be done by you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And ye shall keep the [feast of] unleavened [bread]; for in this same day have I brought your hosts out of the land of Egypt; and ye shall keep this day in your generations [as] an ordinance for ever.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: In the first [month], on the fourteenth day of the month, in the evening, ye shall eat unleavened bread until the one and twentieth day of the month in the evening.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Seven days shall there be no leaven found in your houses; for whoever eateth what is leavened — that soul shall be cut off from the assembly of Israel, whether he be a sojourner, or born in the land.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Ye shall eat nothing leavened: in all your dwellings shall ye eat unleavened bread.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses called all the elders of Israel, and said to them, Seize and take yourselves lambs for your families, and kill the passover.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And take a bunch of hyssop, and dip [it] in the blood that is in the bason, and smear the lintel and the two door-posts with the blood that is in the bason; and none of you shall go out of the door of his house until the morning.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah will pass through to smite the Egyptians; and when he sees the blood on the lintel, and on the two door-posts, Jehovah will pass over the door, and will not suffer the destroyer to come into your houses to smite [you].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And ye shall observe this as an ordinance for thee and for thy sons for ever.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it shall come to pass, when ye are come into the land that Jehovah will give you, as he has promised, that ye shall keep this service.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it shall come to pass, when your children shall say to you, What mean ye by this service?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: that ye shall say, It is a sacrifice of passover to Jehovah, who passed over the houses of the children of Israel in Egypt when he smote the Egyptians and delivered our houses. And the people bowed their heads and worshipped.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the children of Israel went away, and did as Jehovah had commanded Moses and Aaron; so did they.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass that at midnight Jehovah smote all the firstborn in the land of Egypt, from the firstborn of Pharaoh who sat on his throne to the firstborn of the captive that was in the dungeon, and all the firstborn of cattle.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Pharaoh rose up in the night, he, and all his bondmen, and all the Egyptians; and there was a great cry in Egypt; for there was not a house in which there was not one dead.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he called Moses and Aaron in the night, and said, Rise up, go away from among my people, both ye and the children of Israel; and go, serve Jehovah, as ye have said.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Also take your flocks and your herds, as ye have said, and go; and bless me also.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the Egyptians urged the people, to send them out of the land in haste; for they said, We are all dead [men]!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the people took their dough before it was leavened; their kneading-troughs bound up in their clothes upon their shoulders.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the children of Israel had done according to the word of Moses, and they had asked of the Egyptians utensils of silver, and utensils of gold, and clothing.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah had given the people favour in the eyes of the Egyptians, and they gave to them; and they spoiled the Egyptians.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the children of Israel journeyed from Rameses to Succoth, about six hundred thousand on foot [that were] men, besides children.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And a mixed multitude went up also with them; and flocks and herds — very much cattle.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they baked the dough that they brought forth out of Egypt into unleavened cakes, for it was not leavened; for they were driven out of Egypt, and could not wait; neither had they prepared for themselves any food.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the residence of the children of Israel that they resided in Egypt was four hundred and thirty years.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass at the end of the four hundred and thirty years, on that same day it came to pass that all the hosts of Jehovah went out from the land of Egypt.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: It is a night of observance to Jehovah, because of their being brought out from the land of Egypt: that same night is an observance to Jehovah for all the children of Israel in their generations.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah said to Moses and Aaron, This is the ordinance of the passover: No stranger shall eat of it;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but every man's bondman that is bought for money — let him be circumcised: then shall he eat it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: A settler and a hired servant shall not eat it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: In one house shall it be eaten; thou shalt not carry forth any of the flesh abroad out of the house; neither shall ye break a bone thereof.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: All the assembly of Israel shall hold it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when a sojourner sojourneth with thee, and would hold the passover to Jehovah, let all his males be circumcised, and then let him come near and hold it; and he shall be as one that is born in the land; but no uncircumcised person shall eat thereof.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: One law shall be for him that is home-born and for the sojourner that sojourneth among you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And all the children of Israel did as Jehovah had commanded Moses and Aaron; so did they.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass on that same day, [that] Jehovah brought the children of Israel out of the land of Egypt according to their hosts.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah spoke to Moses, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Hallow unto me every firstborn, whatever breaketh open the womb among the children of Israel, of man and of cattle: it is mine.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses said to the people, Remember this day, in which ye came out from Egypt, out of the house of bondage; for with a powerful hand hath Jehovah brought you out from this; and nothing leavened shall be eaten.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Ye come out to-day, in the month Abib.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it shall be when Jehovah hath brought thee into the land of the Canaanite and the Hittite and the Amorite and the Hivite and the Jebusite, which he swore to thy fathers to give thee, a land flowing with milk and honey, that thou shalt keep this service in this month.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Seven days shalt thou eat unleavened bread; and in the seventh day is a feast to Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Unleavened bread shall be eaten the seven days; and leavened bread shall not be seen with thee, neither shall there be leaven seen with thee in all thy borders.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt inform thy son in that day, saying, It is because of what Jehovah did to me when I came out of Egypt.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it shall be for a sign to thee on thy hand, and for a memorial between thine eyes, that the law of Jehovah may be in thy mouth; for with a powerful hand hath Jehovah brought thee out of Egypt.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt keep this ordinance at its set time from year to year.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it shall be when Jehovah hath brought thee into the land of the Canaanites, as he hath sworn to thee and to thy fathers, and hath given it thee,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: that thou shalt offer unto Jehovah all that breaketh open the womb, and every firstling that cometh of cattle which is thine: the males [shall be] Jehovah's.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And every firstling of an ass shalt thou ransom with a lamb; and if thou do not ransom it, thou shalt break its neck; and every firstborn of a man among thy sons shalt thou ransom.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it shall be when thy son asketh thee in time to come, saying, What is this? that thou shalt say to him, With a powerful hand Jehovah brought us out from Egypt, out of the house of bondage.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass, when Pharaoh stubbornly refused to let us go, that Jehovah slew all the firstborn in the land of Egypt, both the firstborn of men and the firstborn of cattle: therefore I sacrifice to Jehovah all that breaketh open the womb — being males; and every firstborn of my children I ransom.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it shall be for a sign on thy hand, and for frontlets between thine eyes, for with a powerful hand Jehovah brought us forth out of Egypt.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass, when Pharaoh let the people go, thatGod did not lead them the way of the land of the Philistines, although that was near; forGod said, That the people may not repent when they see conflict, and return to Egypt.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: AndGod led the people about, the way of the wilderness of the Red Sea; and the children of Israel went arrayed out of the land of Egypt.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses took the bones of Joseph with him; for he had made the children of Israel swear an oath, saying,God will be sure to visit you; then ye shall carry my bones with you hence.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they took their journey from Succoth, and encamped in Etham, at the end of the wilderness.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah went before their face by day in a pillar of a cloud, to lead them [in] the way; and by night in a pillar of fire, to give them light; so that they could go day and night.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The pillar of the cloud did not remove [from] before the people by day, nor the pillar of fire by night.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah spoke to Moses, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Speak unto the children of Israel, that they turn and encamp before Pi-hahiroth, between Migdol and the sea: before Baal-Zephon, opposite to it, shall ye encamp by the sea.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Pharaoh will say of the children of Israel, They are entangled in the land, the wilderness has hemmed them in.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I will harden Pharaoh's heart, that he may pursue after them; and I will glorify myself in Pharaoh, and in all his host; and the Egyptians shall know that I am Jehovah. And they did so.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it was told the king of Egypt that the people had fled; and the heart of Pharaoh and of his bondmen was turned against the people, and they said, Why have we done this, that we have let Israel go from our service?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he yoked his chariot, and took his people with him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he took six hundred chosen chariots, and all the chariots of Egypt, and captains over every one of them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah hardened the heart of Pharaoh king of Egypt, and he pursued after the children of Israel; and the children of Israel had gone out with a high hand.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the Egyptians pursued after them, — all the horses and chariots of Pharaoh, and his horsemen, and his army, and overtook them where they had encamped by the sea, beside Pi-hahiroth, opposite to Baal-Zephon.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Pharaoh approached; and the children of Israel lifted up their eyes, and behold, the Egyptians marched after them; and the children of Israel were much afraid, and cried out to Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they said to Moses, Is it because there were no graves in Egypt, thou hast taken us away to die in the wilderness? why hast thou done this to us, that thou hast led us out of Egypt?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Is not this what we told thee in Egypt, when we said, Let us alone, and we will serve the Egyptians? For [it had been] better for us to serve the Egyptians than to die in the wilderness.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses said to the people, Fear not: stand still, and see the salvation of Jehovah, which he will work for you to-day; for the Egyptians whom ye have seen to-day, ye shall see them again no more for ever.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehovah will fight for you, and ye shall be still.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah said to Moses, Why dost thou cry unto me? Speak unto the children of Israel, that they go forward.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou, lift thy staff, and stretch out thy hand over the sea, and divide it; and the children of Israel shall go on dry [ground] through the midst of the sea.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I, behold, I will harden the hearts of the Egyptians, and they shall pursue after them; and I will glorify myself in Pharaoh and in all his host, in his chariots and in his horsemen.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the Egyptians shall know that I am Jehovah, when I have glorified myself in Pharaoh, in his chariots and in his horsemen.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the Angel ofGod, who went before the camp of Israel, removed and went behind them; and the pillar of the cloud went from before them, and stood behind them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came between the camp of the Egyptians and the camp of Israel; and was a cloud and darkness, and lit up the night; and the one did not come near the other all the night.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses stretched out his hand over the sea; and Jehovah made the sea go [back] by a strong east wind all the night, and made the sea dry [land], and the waters were divided.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the children of Israel went through the midst of the sea on the dry [ground]; and the waters were a wall to them on their right hand and on their left.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the Egyptians pursued and came after them — all Pharaoh's horses, his chariots and his horsemen, into the midst of the sea.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass in the morning watch, that Jehovah looked upon the camp of the Egyptians, in the pillar of fire and of the cloud, and embarrassed the camp of the Egyptians.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he took off their chariot wheels, and caused them to drive with difficulty; and the Egyptians said, Let us flee before Israel, for Jehovah is fighting for them against the Egyptians!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah said to Moses, Stretch out thy hand over the sea, that the waters may return upon the Egyptians, upon their chariots and upon their horsemen.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses stretched out his hand over the sea, and the sea returned to its strength toward the morning; and the Egyptians fled against it; and Jehovah overturned the Egyptians into the midst of the sea.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the waters returned, and covered the chariots and the horsemen of all the host of Pharaoh that had come into the sea after them; there remained not even one of them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the children of Israel walked on dry [ground] through the midst of the sea; and the waters were a wall to them on their right hand and on their left.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thus Jehovah saved Israel that day out of the hand of the Egyptians; and Israel saw the Egyptians dead on the sea-shore.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Israel saw the great power [with] which Jehovah had wrought against the Egyptians; and the people feared Jehovah, and believed in Jehovah, and in Moses his bondman.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then sang Moses and the children of Israel this song to Jehovah, and spoke, saying, I will sing unto Jehovah, for he is highly exalted: The horse and his rider hath he thrown into the sea.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: My strength and song is Jah, and he is become my salvation: This is myGod, and I will glorify him; My father'sGod, and I will extol him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehovah is a man of war; Jehovah, his name.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Pharaoh's chariots and his army hath he cast into the sea; His chosen captains also are drowned in the Red Sea.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The depths covered them; they sank to the bottom as a stone.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thy right hand, Jehovah, is become glorious in power: Thy right hand, Jehovah, hath dashed in pieces the enemy.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And by the greatness of thine excellency thou hast overthrown thine adversaries: Thou sentest forth thy burning wrath, it consumed them as stubble.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And by the breath of thy nostrils the waters were heaped up; The streams stood as a mound; The depths were congealed in the heart of the sea.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The enemy said, I will pursue, I will overtake, I will divide the spoil; my soul shall be sated upon them; I will unsheath my sword, my hand shall dispossess them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou didst blow with thy breath, the sea covered them; They sank as lead in the mighty waters.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Who is like unto thee, Jehovah, among thegods? Who is like unto thee, glorifying thyself in holiness, Fearful [in] praises, doing wonders?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou stretchedst out thy right hand, the earth swallowed them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou by thy mercy hast led forth the people that thou hast redeemed; Thou hast guided them by thy strength unto the abode of thy holiness.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The peoples heard it, they were afraid: A thrill seized the inhabitants of Philistia.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then the princes of Edom were amazed; The mighty men of Moab, trembling hath seized them; All the inhabitants of Canaan melted away.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Fear and dread fall upon them; By the greatness of thine arm they are still as a stone; Till thy people pass over, Jehovah, Till the people pass over that thou hast purchased.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou shalt bring them in, and plant them in the mountain of thine inheritance, The place that thou, Jehovah, hast made thy dwelling, The Sanctuary, Lord, that thy hands have prepared.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehovah shall reign for ever and ever!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For the horse of Pharaoh, with his chariots and with his horsemen, came into the sea, and Jehovah brought again the waters of the sea upon them; and the children of Israel went on dry [ground] through the midst of the sea.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Miriam the prophetess, the sister of Aaron, took the tambour in her hand, and all the women went out after her with tambours and with dances.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Miriam answered them, Sing to Jehovah, for he is highly exalted: The horse and his rider hath he thrown into the sea.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses brought Israel from the Red Sea, and they went out into the wilderness of Shur; and they went three days in the wilderness, and found no water.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they came to Marah, and could not drink the waters of Marah, for they were bitter; therefore the name of it was called Marah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the people murmured against Moses, saying, What shall we drink?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he cried to Jehovah; and Jehovah shewed him wood, and he cast it into the waters, and the waters became sweet. There he made for them a statute and an ordinance; and there he tested them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
