translations.text: And he said, If thou wilt diligently hearken to the voice of Jehovah thyGod, and do what is right in his eyes, and incline thine ears to his commandments, and keep all his statutes, I will put none of the complaints upon thee that I have put upon the Egyptians; for I am Jehovah who healeth thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they came to Elim; and twelve springs of water were there, and seventy palm trees; and they encamped there by the waters.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they journeyed from Elim, and the whole assembly of the children of Israel came into the wilderness of Sin, which is between Elim and Sinai, on the fifteenth day of the second month after their departure out of the land of Egypt.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the whole assembly of the children of Israel murmured against Moses and against Aaron in the wilderness.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the children of Israel said to them, Would that we had died by the hand of Jehovah in the land of Egypt, when we sat by the flesh-pots, when we ate bread to the full; for ye have brought us out into this wilderness, to kill this whole congregation with hunger!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then said Jehovah to Moses, Behold, I will rain bread from heaven for you; and the people shall go out and gather the daily need on its day, that I may prove them, whether they will walk in my law, or not.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it shall come to pass on the sixth day, that they shall prepare what they have brought in; and it shall be twice as much as they shall gather daily.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses and Aaron said to all the children of Israel, In the evening, then shall ye know that Jehovah has brought you out from the land of Egypt;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and in the morning, then shall ye see the glory of Jehovah; for he has heard your murmurings against Jehovah; — and what are we, that ye murmur against us?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses said, When Jehovah gives you in the evening flesh to eat, and in the morning bread to the full; for Jehovah hears your murmurings which ye murmur against him...and what [are] we? your murmurings are not against us, but against Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses spoke to Aaron, Say to all the assembly of the children of Israel, Come near into the presence of Jehovah; for he has heard your murmurings.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass, when Aaron spoke to the whole assembly of the children of Israel, that they turned toward the wilderness, and behold, the glory of Jehovah appeared in the cloud.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah spoke to Moses, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I have heard the murmurings of the children of Israel: speak to them, saying, Between the two evenings ye shall eat flesh, and in the morning ye shall be filled with bread; and ye shall know that I am Jehovah yourGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass in the evening, that quails came up, and covered the camp; and in the morning the dew lay round the camp.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when the dew that lay [round it] was gone up, behold, on the face of the wilderness there was [something] fine, granular, fine as hoar-frost, on the ground.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the children of Israel saw [it], and said one to another, What is it? for they did not know what it was. And Moses said to them, This is the bread which Jehovah has given you to eat.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: This is the thing which Jehovah has commanded: Gather of it every man according to what he can eat, an omer a poll, [according to] the number of your persons: ye shall take every man for those that are in his tent.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the children of Israel did so, and gathered, some much, some little.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they measured with the omer: then he that gathered much had nothing over, and he that gathered little wanted nothing: they had gathered every man according to the measure of his eating.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses said to them, Let no man leave [any] of it until the morning.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But they did not hearken to Moses; and some men left of it until the morning; then worms bred in it and it stank. And Moses was wroth with them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they gathered it every morning, every man as much as he could eat; and when the sun became hot, it melted.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass on the sixth day, that they gathered twice as much bread, two omers for one; and all the princes of the assembly came and told Moses.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to them, This is what Jehovah has said: To-morrow is the rest, the holy sabbath, of Jehovah: bake what ye will bake, and cook what ye will cook; and lay up for yourselves all that remains over, to be kept for the morning.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they laid it up for the morning, as Moses had commanded; and it did not stink, neither was there any worm in it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses said, Eat it to-day; for to-day is sabbath to Jehovah: to-day ye shall not find it in the field.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Six days shall ye gather it; but on the seventh day is sabbath: on it there shall be none.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass on the seventh day that there went out [some] from the people to gather [it], and they found none.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah said to Moses, How long do ye refuse to keep my commandments and my laws?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: See, for Jehovah hath given you the sabbath; therefore he giveth you on the sixth day the bread for two days. Abide every man in his place: let no man go from his place on the seventh day.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the people rested on the seventh day.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the house of Israel called its name Manna; and it was like coriander-seed, white; and the taste of it was like cake with honey.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses said, This is the thing which Jehovah has commanded: Fill an omer of it to be kept for your generations; that they may see the bread that I gave you to eat in the wilderness, when I brought you out of the land of Egypt.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses said to Aaron, Take a pot, and put in it an omer full of manna, and deposit it before Jehovah, to be kept for your generations.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: As Jehovah had commanded Moses, so Aaron deposited it before the Testimony, to be kept.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the children of Israel ate the manna forty years, until they came into an inhabited land: they ate the manna until they came to the borders of the land of Canaan.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now an omer is the tenth [part] of an ephah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And all the assembly of the children of Israel journeyed from the wilderness of Sin, according to their journeys, at the command of Jehovah; and they encamped in Rephidim; and there was no water for the people to drink.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the people contended with Moses, and said, Give us water, that we may drink! And Moses said to them, Why do ye dispute with me? Why do ye tempt Jehovah?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the people thirsted there for water; and the people murmured against Moses, and said, Why is it that thou hast brought us up out of Egypt, to kill us and our children and our cattle with thirst?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses cried to Jehovah, saying, What shall I do with this people? Yet a little, and they will stone me!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah said to Moses, Go on before the people, and take with thee of the elders of Israel, and thy staff with which thou didst smite the river, take in thy hand, and go.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Behold, I will stand before thee there upon the rock on Horeb; and thou shalt strike the rock, and there shall come water out of it, that the people may drink. And Moses did so before the eyes of the elders of Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he called the name of the place Massah, and Meribah, because of the contention of the children of Israel, and because they had tempted Jehovah, saying, Is Jehovah among us, or not?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Amalek came and fought with Israel in Rephidim.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses said to Joshua, Choose us men, and go out, fight with Amalek; to-morrow I will stand on the top of the hill with the staff ofGod in my hand.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Joshua did as Moses had said to him, to fight with Amalek; and Moses, Aaron and Hur went up to the top of the hill.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass when Moses raised his hand, that Israel prevailed; and when he let down his hand, Amalek prevailed.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses' hands were heavy; then they took a stone, and put [it] under him, and he sat on it; and Aaron and Hur supported his hands, one on this side, and one on that side; and his hands were steady until the going down of the sun.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Joshua broke the power of Amalek and his people with the edge of the sword.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah said to Moses, Write this [for] a memorial in the book, and rehearse [it] in the ears of Joshua, that I will utterly blot out the remembrance of Amalek from under the heavens.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses built an altar, and called the name of it Jehovah-nissi.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said, For the hand is on the throne of Jah; Jehovah will have war with Amalek from generation to generation!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jethro the priest of Midian, Moses' father-in-law, heard of all thatGod had done to Moses, and to Israel his people; that Jehovah had brought Israel out of Egypt.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jethro, Moses' father-in-law, took Zipporah, Moses' wife, after he had sent her back,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and her two sons, of whom the name of the one was Gershom — for he said, I have been a sojourner in a foreign land,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: — and the name of the other, Eliezer — For theGod of my father has been my help, and has delivered me from the sword of Pharaoh.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jethro, Moses' father-in-law, came to Moses with his sons and his wife into the wilderness, where he encamped at the mountain ofGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he sent word to Moses: I, thy father-in-law Jethro, am come to thee, and thy wife, and her two sons with her.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses went out to meet his father-in-law, and did obeisance, and kissed him; and they asked each other [after] their welfare, and went into the tent.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses told his father-in-law all that Jehovah had done to Pharaoh and to the Egyptians for Israel's sake; all the trouble that had befallen them on the way, and [how] Jehovah had delivered them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jethro rejoiced for all the goodness that Jehovah had done to Israel; that he had delivered them out of the hand of the Egyptians.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jethro said, Blessed be Jehovah, who has delivered you out of the hand of the Egyptians, and out of the hand of Pharaoh; who has delivered the people from under the hand of the Egyptians.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now I know that Jehovah is greater than allgods; for in the thing in which they acted haughtily [he was] above them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jethro, Moses' father-in-law, took a burnt-offering and sacrifices forGod; and Aaron came, and all the elders of Israel, to eat bread with Moses' father-in-law in the presence ofGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass on the morrow, that Moses sat to judge the people; and the people stood by Moses from the morning to the evening.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses' father-in-law saw all that he did with the people, and said, What is this thing which thou art doing with the people? why dost thou sit alone, and all the people are standing by thee from morning to evening?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses said to his father-in-law, Because the people come to me to enquire ofGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: When they have a matter, they come to me, and I judge between one and another; and I make known [to them] the statutes ofGod, and his laws.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses' father-in-law said to him, The thing that thou art doing is not good.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou wilt be quite exhausted, both thou and this people that is with thee; for the thing is too heavy for thee: thou canst not perform it alone.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Hearken now to my voice: I will give thee counsel, andGod shall be with thee. Be thou for the people withGod, and bring the matters beforeGod;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and teach them the statutes and the laws, and make known to them the way in which they must walk, and the work that they must do.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But do thou provide among all the people able men, such as fearGod, men of truth, hating covetousness; and place [them] over them, chiefs of thousands, chiefs of hundreds, chiefs of fifties, and chiefs of tens,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: that they may judge the people at all times; and it shall be [that] they shall bring to thee every great matter, and that they shall judge every small matter, and they shall lighten [the task] on thee, and they shall bear [it] with thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If thou do this thing, andGod command thee [so], thou wilt be able to endure, and all this people shall also go to their place in peace.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses hearkened to the voice of his father-in-law, and did all that he had said.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses chose able men out of all Israel, and made them heads over the people, chiefs of thousands, chiefs of hundreds, chiefs of fifties, and chiefs of tens.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they judged the people at all times: the hard matters they brought to Moses, but every small matter they judged.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses sent away his father-in-law, and he departed into his land.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: In the third month after the departure of the children of Israel out of the land of Egypt, the same day came they [into] the wilderness of Sinai:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: they departed from Rephidim, and came [into] the wilderness of Sinai, and encamped in the wilderness; and Israel encamped there before the mountain.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses went up toGod, and Jehovah called to him out of the mountain, saying, Thus shalt thou say to the house of Jacob, and tell the children of Israel:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Ye have seen what I have done to the Egyptians, and [how] I have borne you on eagles' wings and brought you to myself.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And now, if ye will hearken to my voice indeed and keep my covenant, then shall ye be my own possession out of all the peoples — for all the earth is mine —

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and ye shall be to me a kingdom of priests, and a holy nation. These are the words which thou shalt speak to the children of Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses came and called the elders of the people, and laid before them all these words which Jehovah had commanded him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And all the people answered together, and said, All that Jehovah has spoken will we do! And Moses brought the words of the people back to Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah said to Moses, Lo, I will come to thee in the cloud's thick darkness, that the people may hear when I speak with thee, and believe thee also for ever. And Moses told the words of the people to Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah said to Moses, Go to the people, and hallow them to-day and to-morrow, and let them wash their clothes;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and let them be ready for the third day; for on the third day Jehovah will come down before the eyes of all the people on mount Sinai.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And set bounds round about the people, saying, Take heed to yourselves, [not] to go up unto the mountain nor touch the border of it: whatever toucheth the mountain shall certainly be put to death:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: not a hand shall touch it, but it shall certainly be stoned, or shot through; whether it be a beast or a man, it shall not live. When the long drawn note of the trumpet soundeth, they shall come up to the mountain.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses came down from the mountain to the people, and hallowed the people; and they washed their clothes.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to the people, Be ready for the third day; do not come near [your] wives.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass on the third day, when it was morning, that there were thunders and lightnings and a heavy cloud on the mountain, and the sound of the trumpet exceeding loud; and the whole people that was in the camp trembled.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses brought the people out of the camp to meet withGod; and they stood at the foot of the mountain.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the whole of mount Sinai smoked, because Jehovah descended on it in fire; and its smoke ascended as the smoke of a furnace; and the whole mountain shook greatly.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the sound of the trumpet increased and became exceeding loud; Moses spoke, andGod answered him by a voice.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah came down on mount Sinai, on the top of the mountain; and Jehovah called Moses to the top of the mountain, and Moses went up.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah said to Moses, Go down, testify to the people that they break not through to Jehovah to gaze, and many of them perish.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the priests also, who come near to Jehovah, shall hallow themselves, lest Jehovah break forth on them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses said to Jehovah, The people cannot come up to mount Sinai; for thou hast testified to us, saying, Set bounds about the mountain, and hallow it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah said to him, Go, descend, and thou shalt come up, thou, and Aaron with thee; but the priests and the people shall not break through to go up to Jehovah, lest he break forth on them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: So Moses went down to the people, and told them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: AndGod spoke all these words, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I am Jehovah thyGod, who have brought thee out of the land of Egypt, out of the house of bondage.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou shalt have no othergods before me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou shalt not make thyself any graven image, or any form of what is in the heavens above, or what is in the earth beneath, or what is in the waters under the earth:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: thou shalt not bow down thyself to them, nor serve them; for I, Jehovah thyGod, am a jealousGod, visiting the iniquity of the fathers upon the sons to the third and to the fourth [generation] of them that hate me,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and shewing mercy unto thousands of them that love me and keep my commandments.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou shalt not idly utter the name of Jehovah thyGod; for Jehovah will not hold him guiltless that idly uttereth his name.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Remember the sabbath day to hallow it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Six days shalt thou labour, and do all thy work;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but the seventh day is the sabbath of Jehovah thyGod: thou shalt not do any work, thou, nor thy son, nor thy daughter, thy bondman, nor thy handmaid, nor thy cattle, nor thy stranger that is within thy gates.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For in six days Jehovah made the heavens and the earth, the sea, and all that is in them, and rested on the seventh day; therefore Jehovah blessed the sabbath day, and hallowed it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Honour thy father and thy mother, that thy days may be prolonged in the land that Jehovah thyGod giveth thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou shalt not kill.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou shalt not commit adultery.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou shalt not steal.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou shalt not bear false witness against thy neighbour.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou shalt not desire thy neighbour's house, thou shalt not desire thy neighbour's wife, nor his bondman, nor his handmaid, nor his ox, nor his ass, nor anything that is thy neighbour's.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And all the people saw the thunderings, and the flames, and the sound of the trumpet, and the mountain smoking; and when the people saw [it], they trembled, and stood afar off,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and said to Moses, Speak thou with us, and we will hear; but let notGod speak with us, lest we die.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses said to the people, Fear not; forGod is come to prove you, and that his fear may be before you, that ye sin not.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the people stood afar off, and Moses drew near to the obscurity whereGod was.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah said to Moses, Thus shalt thou say to the children of Israel: Ye have seen that I have spoken with you from the heavens.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Ye shall not make beside megods of silver, and ye shall not make to yougods of gold.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: An altar of earth shalt thou make unto me, and shalt sacrifice on it thy burnt-offerings, and thy peace-offerings, thy sheep and thine oxen: in all places where I shall make my name to be remembered, I will come unto thee, and bless thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And if thou make me an altar of stone, thou shalt not build it of hewn stone; for if thou lift up thy sharp tool upon it, thou hast profaned it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Neither shalt thou go up by steps unto mine altar, that thy nakedness be not discovered thereon.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And these are the judgments which thou shalt set before them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If thou buy a Hebrew bondman, six years shall he serve; and in the seventh he shall go out free for nothing.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If he came in alone, he shall go out alone: if he had a wife, then his wife shall go out with him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If his master have given him a wife, and she have borne him sons or daughters, the wife and her children shall be her master's, and he shall go out alone.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But if the bondman shall say distinctly, I love my master, my wife, and my children, I will not go free;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: then his master shall bring him before the judges, and shall bring him to the door, or to the door-post; and his master shall bore his ear through with an awl; and he shall be his bondman for ever.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And if a man shall sell his daughter as a handmaid, she shall not go out as the bondmen go out.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If she is unacceptable in the eyes of her master, who had taken her for himself, then shall he let her be ransomed: to sell her unto a foreign people he hath no power, after having dealt unfaithfully with her.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And if he have appointed her unto his son, he shall deal with her after the law of daughters.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If he take himself another, her food, her clothing, and her conjugal rights he shall not diminish.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And if he do not these three things unto her, then shall she go out free without money.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He that striketh a man, so that he die, shall certainly be put to death.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But if he have not lain in wait, andGod have delivered [him] into his hand, I will appoint thee a place to which he shall flee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But if a man act wantonly toward his neighbour, and slay him with guile, thou shalt take him from mine altar, that he may die.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he that striketh his father, or his mother, shall certainly be put to death.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he that stealeth a man, and selleth him, or if he be found in his hand, he shall certainly be put to death.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he that curseth his father, or his mother, shall certainly be put to death.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And if men dispute, and one strike the other with a stone, or with the fist, and he die not, but take to [his] bed,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: — if he rise, and walk abroad upon his staff, then shall he that struck [him] be guiltless; only he shall pay [for] the loss of his time, and shall cause [him] to be thoroughly healed.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And if a man strike his bondman or his handmaid with a staff, and he die under his hand, he shall certainly be avenged.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Only, if he continue [to live] a day or two days, he shall not be avenged; for he is his money.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And if men strive together, and strike a woman with child, so that she be delivered, and no mischief happen, he shall in any case be fined, according as the woman's husband shall impose on him, and shall give it as the judges estimate.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But if mischief happen, then thou shalt give life for life,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: eye for eye, tooth for tooth, hand for hand, foot for foot,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: branding for branding, wound for wound, stripe for stripe.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And if a man strike the eye of his bondman or the eye of his handmaid, and it be marred, he shall let him go for his eye.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And if he knock out his bondman's tooth or his handmaid's tooth, he shall let him go free for his tooth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And if an ox gore a man or a woman, so that they die, then the ox shall certainly be stoned, and its flesh shall not be eaten; but the owner of the ox shall be guiltless.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But if the ox have gored heretofore, and it have been testified to its owner, and he have not kept it in, and it kill a man or a woman, — the ox shall be stoned, and its owner also shall be put to death.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If there be imposed on him a satisfaction, then he shall give the ransom of his life, according to what is imposed on him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Whether it gore a son or gore a daughter, according to this judgment shall it be done to him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If the ox gore a bondman or a handmaid, he shall give to their master thirty shekels of silver, and the ox shall be stoned.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: — And if a man open a pit, or if a man dig a pit, and do not cover it, and an ox or an ass fall into it,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: the owner of the pit shall make it good, shall give money to the owner of them; and the dead [ox] shall be his.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: — And if one man's ox gore his neighbour's ox, and it die, then they shall sell the live ox, and divide the money thereof, and divide the dead also.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Or if it be known that the ox have gored heretofore, and its owner have not kept him in, he shall in any case restore ox for ox; and the dead shall be his.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If a man steal an ox, or a sheep, and kill it, or sell it, he shall restore five oxen for the ox, and four sheep for the sheep.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If the thief be encountered breaking in, and be smitten so that he die, there shall be no blood-guiltiness for him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If the sun be risen on him, there shall be blood-guiltiness for him; he should have made full restitution: if he had nothing, he would have been sold for his theft.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If the stolen thing be actually found alive in his hand, whether it be ox, or ass, or sheep, he shall restore double.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If a man shall cause a field or vineyard to be eaten, and put in his cattle, and pasture in another man's field, of the best of his own field, and of the best of his own vineyard shall he make [it] good.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: — If fire break out, and seize the thorns, and the stacks of corn, or the standing corn, or the field be consumed, he that kindled the fire shall fully make it good.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: — If a man deliver unto his neighbour money or stuff to keep, and it be stolen out of the man's house; if the thief be found, let him restore double;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: if the thief be not found, the master of the house shall be brought before the judges, [to see] if he has not put his hand unto his neighbour's goods.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: As to all manner of fraud, — as to ox, as to ass, as to sheep, as to clothing, as to everything lost, of which [a man] saith, It is this — the cause of both parties shall come before the judges: he whom the judges shall condemn shall restore double to his neighbour.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If a man deliver unto his neighbour an ass, or an ox, or a sheep, or any cattle, to keep, and it die, or be hurt, or driven away, and no man see [it],

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: an oath of Jehovah shall be between them both, that he hath not put his hand unto his neighbour's goods; and the owner of it shall accept it, and he shall not make [it] good.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But if it have been stolen from him, he shall make [it] good unto its owner.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If it have been torn in pieces, let him bring it [as] witness: he shall not make good what was torn.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: — And if a man borrow anything of his neighbour, and it be hurt, or die, its owner not being with it, he shall fully make it good;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: if the owner thereof be with it, he shall not make [it] good; if it be a hired [thing], it came for its hire.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And if a man seduce a virgin that is not betrothed, and lie with her, he shall certainly endow her, to be his wife.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If her father utterly refuse to give her unto him, he shall weigh money according to the dowry of virgins.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: — Thou shalt not suffer a witch to live.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: — Every one that lieth with a beast shall certainly be put to death.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: — He that sacrificeth to [any]god, save to Jehovah only, shall be devoted to destruction.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou shalt neither vex a stranger, nor oppress him; for ye have been strangers in the land of Egypt.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Ye shall not afflict any widow or fatherless child.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If thou afflict him in any way, if he cry at all unto me, I will certainly hear his cry;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and my anger shall burn, and I will slay you with the sword; and your wives shall be widows, and your children fatherless.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: — If thou lend money to my people, the poor with thee, thou shalt not be to him as a usurer: ye shall charge him no interest.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: — If thou at all take thy neighbour's garment in pledge, thou shalt return it to him before the sun goes down;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for that is his only covering, his garment for his skin: on what shall he lie down? And it shall come to pass, when he crieth unto me, that I will hear; for I am gracious.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou shalt not revile the judges, nor curse a prince amongst thy people.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: — Thou shalt not delay the fulness of thy [threshing-floor] and the outflow of thy [winepress]. The firstborn of thy sons shalt thou give unto me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Likewise shalt thou do with thy calf, with thy sheep: seven days shall it be with its dam; on the eighth day thou shalt give it me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: — And ye shall be holy men unto me; and ye shall not eat flesh torn in the field: ye shall cast it to the dog.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou shalt not accept a false report; extend not thy hand to the wicked, to be an unrighteous witness.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou shalt not follow the multitude for evil; neither shalt thou answer in a cause, to go after the multitude to pervert [judgment].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Neither shalt thou favour a poor man in his cause.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: — If thou meet thine enemy's ox or his ass going astray, thou shalt certainly bring it back to him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If thou see the ass of him that hateth thee lying under its burden, beware of leaving [it] to him: thou shalt certainly loosen [it] with him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou shalt not pervert the judgment of thy poor in his cause.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou shalt keep far from the cause of falsehood; and the innocent and righteous slay not; for I will not justify the wicked.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt take no bribe; for the bribe blindeth those whose eyes are open, and perverteth the words of the righteous.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the stranger thou shalt not oppress; for ye know the spirit of the stranger, for ye have been strangers in the land of Egypt.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And six years thou shalt sow thy land, and gather in its produce;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but in the seventh thou shalt let it rest and lie [fallow], that the poor of thy people may eat [of it]; and what they leave, the beasts of the field shall eat. In like manner thou shalt deal with thy vineyard, and with thine olive-tree.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: — Six days thou shalt do thy work, but on the seventh day thou shalt rest; that thine ox and thine ass may rest, and the son of thy handmaid, and the stranger may be refreshed.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And ye shall be on your guard as to everything that I have said unto you; and shall make no mention of the name of othergods — it shall not be heard in thy mouth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thrice in the year thou shalt celebrate a feast to me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou shalt keep the feast of unleavened bread, (thou shalt eat unleavened bread seven days, as I have commanded thee, in the time appointed of the month Abib; for in it thou camest out from Egypt; and none shall appear in my presence empty;)

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and the feast of harvest, the first-fruits of thy labours which thou hast sown in the field, and the feast of in-gathering, at the end of the year, when thou gatherest in thy labours out of the field.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Three times in the year all thy males shall appear in the presence of the Lord Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou shalt not offer the blood of my sacrifice with leavened bread; neither shall the fat of my feast remain all night until the morning.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The first of the first-fruits of thy land thou shalt bring into the house of Jehovah thyGod. Thou shalt not boil a kid in its mother's milk.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Behold, I send an Angel before thee, to keep thee in the way, and to bring thee to the place that I have prepared.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Be careful in his presence, and hearken unto his voice: do not provoke him, for he will not forgive your transgressions; for my name is in him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But if thou shalt diligently hearken unto his voice, and do all that I shall say, then I will be an enemy to thine enemies, and an adversary to thine adversaries.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For mine Angel shall go before thee, and bring thee in unto the Amorites and the Hittites and the Perizzites and the Canaanites, the Hivites and the Jebusites; and I will cut them off.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou shalt not bow down to theirgods, nor serve them, nor do after their deeds; but thou shalt utterly destroy them, and utterly shatter their statues.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And ye shall serve Jehovah yourGod; and he shall bless thy bread and thy water; and I will take sickness away from thy midst.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: There shall nothing cast their young, nor be barren, in thy land; the number of thy days will I fulfil.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I will send my fear before thee, and confound every people to which thou comest, and will make all thine enemies turn their back to thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I will send hornets before thee, which shall drive out the Hivite, the Canaanite, and the Hittite, from before thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I will not drive them out from before thee in one year: lest the land become desolate, and the beast of the field multiply against thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: By little and little I will drive them out from before thee, until thou art fruitful, and possess the land.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I will set thy bounds from the Red Sea even unto the sea of the Philistines, and from the wilderness unto the river; for I will give the inhabitants of the land into your hand, that thou mayest dispossess them from before thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou shalt make no covenant with them, nor with theirgods.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They shall not dwell in thy land, lest they make thee sin against me; for if thou serve theirgods, it is sure to be a snare unto thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to Moses, Go up to Jehovah, thou and Aaron, Nadab and Abihu, and seventy of the elders of Israel; and worship afar off.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And let Moses alone come near Jehovah; but they shall not come near; neither shall the people go up with him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses came and told the people all the words of Jehovah, and all the judgments; and all the people answered with one voice, and said, All the words that Jehovah has said will we do!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses wrote all the words of Jehovah, and rose up early in the morning, and built an altar under the mountain, and twelve pillars, according to the twelve tribes of Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he sent the youths of the children of Israel, and they offered up burnt-offerings, and sacrificed sacrifices of peace-offering of bullocks to Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses took half the blood, and put [it] in basons; and half of the blood he sprinkled on the altar.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he took the book of the covenant, and read [it] in the ears of the people; and they said, All that Jehovah has said will we do, and obey!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses took the blood, and sprinkled [it] on the people, and said, Behold the blood of the covenant that Jehovah has made with you concerning all these words.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses and Aaron, Nadab and Abihu, and seventy of the elders of Israel went up;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and they saw theGod of Israel; and there was under his feet as it were work of transparent sapphire, and as it were the form of heaven for clearness.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And on the nobles of the children of Israel he laid not his hand: they sawGod, and ate and drank.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah said to Moses, Come up to me into the mountain, and be there; and I will give thee the tables of stone, and the law, and the commandment that I have written, for their instruction.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses rose up, and Joshua his attendant; and Moses went up to the mountain ofGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to the elders, Wait here for us, until we return to you; and behold, Aaron and Hur are with you: if any man have any matter, let him come before them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses went up to the mountain, and the cloud covered the mountain.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the glory of Jehovah abode on mount Sinai, and the cloud covered it six days; and on the seventh day he called to Moses out of the midst of the cloud.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the appearance of the glory of Jehovah was like a consuming fire on the top of the mountain, before the eyes of the children of Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses went into the midst of the cloud, and ascended the mountain. And Moses was on the mountain forty days and forty nights.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah spoke to Moses, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Speak unto the children of Israel, that they bring me a heave-offering: of every one whose heart prompteth him, ye shall take my heave-offering.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And this is the heave-offering that ye shall take of them: gold, and silver, and copper,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and blue, and purple, and scarlet, and byssus, and goats' [hair],

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and rams' skins dyed red, and badgers' skins; and acacia-wood;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: oil for the light; spices for the anointing oil, and for the incense of fragrant drugs;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: onyx stones, and stones to be set in the ephod, and in the breastplate.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they shall make me a sanctuary, that I may dwell among them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: According to all that I shall shew thee, the pattern of the tabernacle, and the pattern of all the utensils thereof, even so shall ye make [it].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they shall make an ark of acacia-wood; two cubits and a half the length thereof, and a cubit and a half the breadth thereof, and a cubit and a half the height thereof.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt overlay it with pure gold: inside and outside shalt thou overlay it; and shalt make upon it a border of gold round about.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And cast four rings of gold for it, and put [them] at the four corners thereof, that two rings may be upon the one side thereof and two rings upon the other side thereof.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And make staves of acacia-wood and overlay them with gold.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And put the staves into the rings upon the sides of the ark, that the ark may be borne with them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The staves shall be in the rings of the ark: they shall not come out from it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt put into the ark the testimony that I shall give thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt make a mercy-seat of pure gold: two cubits and a half the length thereof, and a cubit and a half the breadth thereof.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt make two cherubim of gold; [of] beaten work shalt thou make them, at the two ends of the mercy-seat.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And make one cherub at the end of the one side, and one cherub at the end of the other side; out of the mercy-seat shall ye make the cherubim at the two ends thereof.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the cherubim shall stretch out [their] wings over it, covering over with their wings the mercy-seat, and their faces opposite to one another: toward the mercy-seat shall the faces of the cherubim be [turned].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt put the mercy-seat above on the ark, and shalt put in the ark the testimony that I shall give thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And there will I meet with thee, and will speak with thee from above the mercy-seat, from between the two cherubim which are upon the ark of the testimony, everything that I will give thee in commandment unto the children of Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt make a table of acacia-wood, two cubits the length thereof, and a cubit the breadth thereof, and a cubit and a half the height thereof.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt overlay it with pure gold, and make upon it a border of gold round about.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt make for it a margin of a handbreadth round about, and shalt make a border of gold for the margin thereof round about.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt make for it four rings of gold, and put the rings at the four corners that are on the four feet thereof.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Close to the margin shall the rings be, as receptacles of the staves to carry the table.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt make the staves of acacia-wood, and overlay them with gold; and the table shall be carried upon them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt make the dishes thereof, and cups thereof, and goblets thereof, and bowls thereof, with which to pour out: of pure gold shalt thou make them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt set upon the table shewbread before me continually.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt make a lamp-stand of pure gold; [of] beaten work shall the lamp-stand be made: its base and its shaft, its cups, its knobs, and its flowers shall be of the same.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And six branches shall come out of the sides thereof — three branches of the lamp-stand out of one side thereof, and three branches of the lamp-stand out of the other side thereof;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: three cups shaped like almonds in the one branch, a knob and a flower: and three cups shaped like almonds in the other branch, a knob and a flower: so in the six branches that come out of the lamp-stand.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And in the lamp-stand four cups shaped like almonds, its knobs and its flowers;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and a knob under two branches of it, and [again] a knob under two branches of it, and [again] a knob under two branches of it, for the six branches that proceed out of the lamp-stand.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Their knobs and their branches shall be of itself — all of one beaten work of pure gold.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt make the seven lamps thereof, and they shall light the lamps thereof, that they may shine out before it;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and the snuffers thereof, and the snuff-trays thereof, of pure gold.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Of a talent of pure gold shall they make it, with all these utensils.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And see that thou make [them] according to their pattern, which hath been shewn to thee in the mountain.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt make the tabernacle [with] ten curtains of twined byssus, and blue, and purple, and scarlet: with cherubim of artistic work shalt thou make them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The length of one curtain shall be twenty-eight cubits, and the breadth of one curtain four cubits — one measure for all the curtains.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Five of the curtains shall be coupled one to another, and [the other] five curtains coupled one to another.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt make loops of blue on the edge of the one curtain at the end of the coupling; and likewise shalt thou make [them] in the edge of the outermost curtain in the other coupling.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Fifty loops shalt thou make in the one curtain, and fifty loops shalt thou make at the end of the curtain in the other coupling: the loops shall be opposite to one another.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt make fifty clasps of gold, and couple the curtains together with the clasps, that the tabernacle may be one [whole].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt make curtains of goats' [hair] for a tent over the tabernacle: eleven curtains shalt thou make them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The length of one curtain shall be thirty cubits, and the breadth of one curtain four cubits — one measure for the eleven curtains.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt couple five of the curtains by themselves, and six of the curtains by themselves, and shalt double the sixth curtain in the front of the tent.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt make fifty loops on the edge of the outermost curtain of the coupling, and fifty loops on the edge of the curtain in the other coupling.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt make fifty clasps of copper, and put the clasps into the loops, and couple the tent, that it may be one [whole].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And that which remaineth hanging over of the curtains of the tent, the half curtain that remaineth, shall hang over the rear of the tabernacle.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the cubit on the one side, and the cubit on the other side of that which remaineth in the length of the curtains of the tent, shall hang over the sides of the tabernacle on this side and on that side, to cover it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt make a covering for the tent of rams' skins dyed red, and a covering of badgers' skins over [that].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the boards for the tabernacle thou shalt make of acacia-wood, standing up;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: ten cubits the length of the board, and a cubit and a half the breadth of one board.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: One board shall have two tenons, connected one with the other: thus shalt thou make for all the boards of the tabernacle.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt make the boards for the tabernacle, twenty boards on the south side southward.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt make forty bases of silver under the twenty boards; two bases under one board for its two tenons, and two bases under another board for its two tenons.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And for the other side of the tabernacle on the north side there shall be twenty boards.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And their forty bases of silver; two bases under one board, and two bases under another board.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And for the rear of the tabernacle westward thou shalt make six boards.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And two boards shalt thou make for the corners of the tabernacle at the rear;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and they shall be joined beneath, and together shall be united at the top thereof to one ring: thus shall it be for them both; they shall be for the two corners.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And there shall be eight boards, and their bases, of silver, sixteen bases; two bases under one board, and two bases under another board.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt make bars of acacia-wood; five for the boards of the one side of the tabernacle,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and five bars for the boards of the other side of the tabernacle, and five bars for the boards of the side of the tabernacle at the rear westward;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and the middle bar in the midst of the boards reaching from one end to the other.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt overlay the boards with gold, and make [of] gold their rings, the receptacles of the bars, and shalt overlay the bars with gold.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt set up the tabernacle according to its fashion, as hath been shewn thee on the mountain.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt make a veil of blue, and purple, and scarlet, and twined byssus; of artistic work shall it be made, with cherubim.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt attach it to four pillars of acacia-wood overlaid with gold, their hooks of gold; they shall be on four bases of silver.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt bring the veil under the clasps, and bring in thither, inside the veil, the ark of the testimony; and the curtain shall make a division to you between the holy [place] and the holiest of all.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt put the mercy-seat on the ark of the testimony in the holiest of all.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt set the table outside the veil, and the lamp-stand opposite to the table on the side of the tabernacle southward; and thou shalt put the table on the north side.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt make for the entrance of the tent a curtain of blue, and purple, and scarlet, and twined byssus, of embroidery.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt make for the curtain five pillars of acacia[-wood], and overlay them with gold; their hooks shall be of gold; and thou shalt cast five bases of copper for them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt make the altar of acacia-wood, five cubits the length, and five cubits the breadth; the altar shall be square; and the height thereof three cubits.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt make its horns at the four corners thereof; its horns shall be of itself; and thou shalt overlay it with copper.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt make its pots to cleanse it of the fat, its shovels, and its bowls, and its forks, and its firepans; for all the utensils thereof thou shalt employ copper.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt make for it a grating of network of copper; and on the net shalt thou make four copper rings at its four corners;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and thou shalt put it under the ledge of the altar beneath, and the net shall be to the very middle of the altar.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt make staves for the altar, staves of acacia-wood, and overlay them with copper.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And its staves shall be put into the rings, that the staves may be on both sides of the altar, when it is carried.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Hollow with boards shalt thou make it: as it hath been shewn thee on the mountain, so shall they make [it].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt make the court of the tabernacle. On the south side, southward, hangings for the court of twined byssus; a hundred cubits the length for the one side,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and the twenty pillars thereof, and their twenty bases of copper, the hooks of the pillars and their connecting-rods of silver.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And likewise on the north side in length, hangings a hundred [cubits] long, and its twenty pillars, and their twenty bases of copper; the hooks of the pillars and their connecting-rods of silver.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: — And the breadth of the court on the west side, hangings of fifty cubits; their pillars ten, and their bases ten.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: — And the breadth of the court on the east side, eastward, fifty cubits;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: the hangings on the one wing, of fifteen cubits; their pillars three, and their bases three.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And on the other wing hangings of fifteen [cubits]; their pillars three, and their bases three.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: — And for the gate of the court a curtain of twenty cubits, of blue, and purple, and scarlet, and twined byssus, embroidered with needlework; their pillars four, and their bases four.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: All the pillars of the court round about shall be fastened together with [rods of] silver; their hooks of silver, and their bases of copper.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The length of the court shall be a hundred cubits, and the breadth fifty everywhere, and the height five cubits of twined byssus; and their bases of copper.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: All the utensils of the tabernacle for the service thereof and all the pegs thereof, and all the pegs of the court shall be of copper.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt command the children of Israel, that they bring thee olive oil, pure, beaten, for the light, to light the lamp continually.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: In the tent of meeting outside the veil, which is before the testimony, Aaron and his sons shall dress them from evening to morning before Jehovah: [it is] an everlasting statute, for their generations, on the part of the children of Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt take thee Aaron thy brother, and his sons with him, from among the children of Israel, that he may serve me as priest — Aaron, Nadab and Abihu, Eleazar and Ithamar, Aaron's sons.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt make holy garments for Aaron thy brother, for glory and for ornament.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt speak with all [that are] wise-hearted, whom I have filled with the spirit of wisdom, that they may make Aaron's garments to hallow him, that he may serve me as priest.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And these are the garments which they shall make: a breastplate, and an ephod, and a cloak, and a checkered vest, a turban, and a girdle; and they shall make holy garments for Aaron thy brother, and his sons, that he may serve me as priest.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they shall take gold, and blue, and purple, and scarlet, and twined byssus,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and shall make the ephod of gold, blue, and purple, scarlet and twined byssus, of artistic work.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: It shall have two shoulder-pieces joined at the two ends thereof, where it is joined together.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the girdle of the ephod, which is upon it, shall be of the same, according to its work of gold, blue, and purple, and scarlet and twined byssus.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt take two onyx stones, and engrave on them the names of the children of Israel:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: six of their names on the one stone, and the six names of the rest on the other stone, according to their birth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: According to the work of an engraver in stone, as the engravings of a seal, shalt thou engrave the two stones with the names of the sons of Israel; surrounded by enclosures of gold shalt thou make them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt put the two stones upon the shoulder-pieces of the ephod [as] stones of memorial for the children of Israel; and Aaron shall bear their names before Jehovah upon his two shoulders for a memorial.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt make enclosures of gold;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and two chains of pure gold; of laced work shalt thou make them, of wreathen work, and fasten the wreathen chains to the enclosures.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt make the breastplate of judgment of artistic work, like the work of the ephod thou shalt make it; of gold, blue, and purple, and scarlet, and twined byssus shalt thou make it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Square shall it be, doubled; a span the length thereof, and a span the breadth thereof.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt set in it settings of stones — four rows of stones: [one] row, a sardoin, a topaz, and an emerald — the first row;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and the second row, a carbuncle, a sapphire, and a diamond;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and the third row, an opal, an agate, and an amethyst;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and the fourth row, a chrysolite, and an onyx, and a jasper; enclosed in gold shall they be in their settings.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the stones shall be according to the names of the children of Israel, twelve, according to their names, engraved as a seal: every one according to his name shall they be for the twelve tribes.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt make on the breastplate chains of laced work, of wreathen work, of pure gold.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt make on the breastplate two rings of gold, and shalt put the two rings on the two ends of the breastplate.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt put the two wreathen [cords] of gold in the two rings on the ends of the breastplate;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and the two ends of the two wreathen [cords] thou shalt fasten to the two enclosures, and shalt put [them] on the shoulder-pieces of the ephod, on the front thereof.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt make two rings of gold, and shalt put them on the two ends of the breastplate, on the border thereof, which faceth the ephod inwards.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And two rings of gold shalt thou make, and shalt put them upon the two shoulder-pieces of the ephod underneath, to the front thereof just by the coupling thereof, above the girdle of the ephod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they shall bind the breastplate with its rings to the rings of the ephod with a lace of blue, that it may be above the girdle of the ephod, and that the breastplate be not loosed from the ephod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Aaron shall bear the names of the children of Israel in the breastplate of judgment on his heart, when he goes in to the sanctuary, for a memorial before Jehovah continually.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt put into the breastplate of judgment the Urim and the Thummim, that they may be upon Aaron's heart when he goeth in before Jehovah; and Aaron shall bear the judgment of the children of Israel upon his heart before Jehovah continually.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt make the cloak of the ephod all of blue.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And its opening for the head shall be in the midst thereof; there shall be a binding of woven work at its opening round about; as the opening of a coat of mail, it shall be in it — it shall not rend.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And on the skirts thereof thou shalt make pomegranates of blue, and purple, and scarlet, round about the skirts thereof; and bells of gold between them round about:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: a golden bell and a pomegranate, a golden bell and a pomegranate, in the skirts of the cloak round about.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it shall be on Aaron for service; that his sound may be heard when he goeth into the sanctuary before Jehovah, and when he cometh out, that he may not die.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt make a thin plate of pure gold, and engrave on it, as the engravings of a seal, Holiness to Jehovah!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt put it on a lace of blue, and it shall be upon the turban — upon the front of the turban shall it be.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it shall be upon Aaron's forehead, and Aaron shall bear the iniquity of the holy things, which the children of Israel shall hallow in all gifts of their holy things; and it shall be continually on his forehead, that they may be accepted before Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt weave the vest of byssus; and thou shalt make a turban of byssus; and thou shalt make a girdle of embroidery.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And for Aaron's sons thou shalt make vests; and thou shalt make for them girdles; and high caps shalt thou make for them, for glory and for ornament.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt clothe with them Aaron thy brother, and his sons with him; and shalt anoint them, and consecrate them, and hallow them, that they may serve me as priests.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt make them linen trousers to cover the flesh of nakedness; from the loins even to the hips shall they reach.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they shall be upon Aaron and his sons when they enter into the tent of meeting, or when they come near to the altar to serve in the sanctuary; that they may not bear iniquity and die — an everlasting statute for him and his seed after him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And this is the thing which thou shalt do to them to hallow them, that they may serve me as priests: take one young bullock, and two rams without blemish,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and unleavened bread, and unleavened cakes mingled with oil, and unleavened wafers anointed with oil — of wheaten flour shalt thou make them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt put them into one basket, and present them in the basket, with the bullock and the two rams.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Aaron and his sons thou shalt bring near the entrance of the tent of meeting, and shalt bathe them with water.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt take the garments, and clothe Aaron with the vest, and the cloak of the ephod, and the ephod, and the breastplate, and shalt gird him with the girdle of the ephod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt put the turban upon his head, and fasten the holy diadem to the turban,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and shalt take the anointing oil, and pour [it] on his head, and anoint him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt bring his sons near, and clothe them with the vests.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt gird them with the girdle — Aaron and his sons, and bind the high caps on them; and the priesthood shall be theirs for an everlasting statute; and thou shalt consecrate Aaron and his sons.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt present the bullock before the tent of meeting; and Aaron and his sons shall lay their hands upon the head of the bullock;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and thou shalt slaughter the bullock before Jehovah, at the entrance of the tent of meeting;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and thou shalt take of the blood of the bullock, and put it on the horns of the altar with thy finger, and shalt pour all the blood at the bottom of the altar.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt take all the fat that covereth the inwards, and the net of the liver, and the two kidneys, and the fat that is upon them, and burn them upon the altar.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the flesh of the bullock, and its skin, and its dung, shalt thou burn with fire outside the camp: it is a sin-offering.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt take one of the rams, and Aaron and his sons shall put their hands upon the head of the ram;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and thou shalt slaughter the ram, and shalt take its blood, and sprinkle [it] on the altar round about.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt cut up the ram into its pieces, and wash its inwards, and its legs, and put [them] upon its pieces, and upon its head;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and thou shalt burn the whole ram upon the altar: it is a burnt-offering to Jehovah — a sweet odour; it is an offering by fire to Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt take the second ram, and Aaron and his sons shall lay their hands upon the head of the ram;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and thou shalt slaughter the ram, and take of its blood, and put [it] on the tip of the [right] ear of Aaron, and on the tip of the right ear of his sons, and on the thumb of their right hand, and on the great toe of their right foot; and thou shalt sprinkle the blood upon the altar round about.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt take of the blood that is upon the altar, and of the anointing oil, and sprinkle [it] on Aaron, and on his garments, and on his sons, and on the garments of his sons with him; and he shall be hallowed, and his garments, and his sons, and his sons' garments with him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Also of the ram shalt thou take the fat, and the fat-tail, and the fat that covereth the inwards, and the net of the liver, and the two kidneys, and the fat that is upon them, and the right shoulder — for it is a ram of consecration —

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and one loaf of bread, and one cake of oiled bread, and one wafer out of the basket of the unleavened [bread] that is before Jehovah;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and thou shalt put all this in the hands of Aaron, and in the hands of his sons, and shalt wave them as a wave-offering before Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt receive them of their hand and burn [them] upon the altar over the burnt-offering, for a sweet odour before Jehovah: it is an offering by fire to Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt take the breast of the ram of consecration which is for Aaron, and wave it as a wave-offering before Jehovah; and it shall be thy part.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt hallow the breast of the wave-offering, and the shoulder of the heave-offering, that hath been waved and heaved up, of the ram of the consecration, of that which is for Aaron, and of [that] which is for his sons.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they shall be for Aaron and his sons, as an everlasting statute, on the part of the children of Israel; for it is a heave-offering; and it shall be a heave-offering on the part of the children of Israel of the sacrifices of their peace-offerings, [as] their heave-offering to Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the holy garments of Aaron shall be his sons' after him, to be anointed therein, and to be consecrated in them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The son that is priest in his stead shall put them on seven days, when he cometh into the tent of meeting to serve in the sanctuary.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt take the ram of the consecration, and boil its flesh in a holy place.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Aaron and his sons shall eat the flesh of the ram, and the bread that is in the basket, at the entrance of the tent of meeting.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They shall eat the things with which the atonement was made, to consecrate [and] to hallow them; but a stranger shall not eat [of them], for they are holy.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And if [any] of the flesh of the consecration, and of the bread, remain until the morning, then thou shalt burn the remainder with fire: it shall not be eaten, for it is holy.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thus shalt thou do to Aaron, and to his sons, according to all that I have commanded thee: seven days shalt thou consecrate them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt offer every day a bullock as a sin-offering for atonement; and the altar shalt thou cleanse from sin, by making atonement for it, and shalt anoint it, to hallow it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Seven days shalt thou make atonement for the altar and hallow it; and the altar shall be most holy: whatever toucheth the altar shall be holy.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And this is what thou shalt offer upon the altar — two lambs of the first year, day by day continually.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The one lamb thou shalt offer in the morning; and the other lamb thou shalt offer between the two evenings.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And with the one lamb a tenth part of wheaten flour mingled with beaten oil, a fourth part of a hin; and a drink-offering, a fourth part of a hin of wine.
