translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the second lamb shalt thou offer between the two evenings; as the oblation in the morning, and as its drink-offering shalt thou offer with this, for a sweet odour, an offering by fire to Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: It shall be a continual burnt-offering throughout your generations at the entrance of the tent of meeting before Jehovah, where I will meet with you, to speak there with thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And there will I meet with the children of Israel; and it shall be hallowed by my glory.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I will hallow the tent of meeting, and the altar; and I will hallow Aaron and his sons, that they may serve me as priests.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I will dwell in the midst of the children of Israel, and will be theirGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they shall know that I am Jehovah theirGod, who have brought them forth out of the land of Egypt, to dwell in their midst: I am Jehovah theirGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt make an altar for the burning of incense: of acacia-wood shalt thou make it;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: a cubit the length thereof, and a cubit the breadth thereof — square shall it be; and two cubits its height; of itself shall be its horns.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt overlay it with pure gold, the top thereof, and the sides thereof round about, and the horns thereof; and thou shalt make upon it a border of gold round about.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And two rings of gold shalt thou make for it under its border; by its two corners shalt thou make [them], on the two sides thereof; and they shall be for receptacles for the staves, with which to carry it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt make the staves of acacia-wood, and overlay them with gold.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt put it in front of the veil which is before the ark of the testimony in front of the mercy-seat which is over the testimony, where I will meet with thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Aaron shall burn thereon fragrant incense: every morning, when he dresseth the lamps, he shall burn the incense.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when Aaron lighteth the lamps between the two evenings, he shall burn the incense — a continual incense before Jehovah throughout your generations.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Ye shall offer up no strange incense thereon, nor burnt-offering, nor oblation; neither shall ye pour drink-offering thereon.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Aaron shall make atonement for its horns once in the year: with the blood of the sin-offering of atonement shall he make atonement for it, once in the year, throughout your generations: it is most holy to Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah spoke to Moses, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: When thou shalt take the sum of the children of Israel according to those of them that are numbered, then shall they give every man a ransom for his soul to Jehovah on their being numbered, that there be no plague among them on their being numbered.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: This shall they give — every one that passeth among them that are numbered — half a shekel after the shekel of the sanctuary, — twenty gerahs the shekel; a half shekel shall be the heave-offering for Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Every one that passeth among those that are numbered, from twenty years old and above, shall give the heave-offering of Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The rich shall not give more, and the poor shall not give less than half a shekel, when ye give the heave-offering of Jehovah, to make atonement for your souls.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt take the atonement-money of the children of Israel, and devote it to the service of the tent of meeting; and it shall be a memorial to the children of Israel before Jehovah, to make atonement for your souls.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah spoke to Moses, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou shalt also make a laver of copper, and its stand of copper, for washing; and thou shalt put it between the tent of meeting and the altar, and shalt put water in it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Aaron and his sons shall wash their hands and their feet out of it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: When they go into the tent of meeting, they shall wash with water, that they may not die; or when they come near to the altar to serve, to burn an offering by fire to Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they shall wash their hands and their feet, that they may not die; and it shall be an everlasting statute for them, for him and for his seed throughout their generations.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah spoke to Moses, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou, take best spices — of liquid myrrh five hundred [shekels], and of sweet cinnamon the half — two hundred and fifty, and of sweet myrtle two hundred and fifty,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and of cassia five hundred, after the shekel of the sanctuary, and of olive oil a hin;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and make of it an oil of holy ointment, a perfume of perfumery after the work of the perfumer: it shall be the holy anointing oil.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt anoint the tent of meeting with it, and the ark of the testimony,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and the table and all its utensils, and the lamp-stand and its utensils, and the altar of incense,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and the altar of burnt-offering and all its utensils, and the laver and its stand.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt hallow them, that they may be most holy: whatever toucheth them shall be holy.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Aaron and his sons thou shalt anoint, and shalt hallow them, that they may serve me as priests.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt speak to the children of Israel, saying, A holy anointing oil shall this be unto me throughout your generations.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Upon man's flesh shall it not be poured, neither shall ye make [any] like it, after the preparation of it: it is holy — holy shall it be unto you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Whoever compoundeth [any] like it, or whoever putteth [any] of it upon any strange thing, shall be cut off from his peoples.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah said to Moses, Take fragrant drugs — stacte, and onycha, and galbanum — fragrant drugs and pure frankincense; in like proportions shall it be.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt make it into incense, a perfume, after the work of the perfumer, salted, pure, holy.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt beat [some] of it to powder, and put [some] of it before the testimony in the tent of meeting, where I will meet with thee: it shall be unto you most holy.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the incense that thou shalt make, ye shall not make for yourselves according to the proportions of it; it shall be unto thee holy to Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Whoever maketh like unto it, to smell it, shall be cut off from his peoples.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah spoke to Moses, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: See, I have called by name Bezaleel the son of Uri, the son of Hur, of the tribe of Judah,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and have filled him with the spirit ofGod, in wisdom, and in understanding, and in knowledge, and in every work,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: to devise artistic work — to work in gold, and in silver, and in copper,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and in cutting of stones, for setting, and for carving of timber — to work in all manner of work.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I, behold, I have given with him Aholiab the son of Ahisamach, of the tribe of Dan; and in the heart of every one that is wise-hearted have I given wisdom, that they may make all that I have commanded thee —

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: the tent of meeting and the ark of the testimony, and the mercy-seat that is thereupon, and all the utensils of the tent,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and the table and its utensils, and the pure lamp-stand and all its utensils, and the altar of incense;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and the altar of burnt-offering and all its utensils, and the laver and its stand;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and the garments of service, both the holy garments of Aaron the priest, and the garments of his sons, to serve as priests;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and the anointing oil, and the incense of fragrant drugs for the sanctuary: according to all that I have commanded thee shall they do.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah spoke to Moses, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou, speak thou unto the children of Israel, saying, Surely my sabbaths shall ye keep; for this is a sign between me and you throughout your generations, that ye may know that it is I, Jehovah, who do hallow you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Keep the sabbath, therefore; for it is holy unto you; every one that profaneth it shall certainly be put to death: yea, whoever doeth work on it, that soul shall be cut off from among his peoples.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Six days shall work be done; but on the seventh day is the sabbath of rest, holy to Jehovah: whoever doeth work on the sabbath day shall certainly be put to death.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the children of Israel shall keep the sabbath, to observe the sabbath throughout their generations — [it is] an everlasting covenant.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: It shall be a sign between me and the children of Israel for ever; for [in] six days Jehovah made the heavens and the earth, and on the seventh day he rested, and was refreshed.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he gave to Moses, when he had ended speaking with him on mount Sinai, the two tables of testimony, tables of stone, written with the finger ofGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when the people saw that Moses delayed to come down from the mountain, the people collected together to Aaron, and said to him, Up, make us agod, who will go before us; for this Moses, the man that has brought us up out of the land of Egypt, — we do not know what is become of him!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Aaron said to them, Break off the golden rings that are in the ears of your wives, of your sons, and of your daughters, and bring [them] to me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then all the people broke off the golden rings that were in their ears, and brought [them] to Aaron.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he took [them] out of their hand, and fashioned it with a chisel and made of it a molten calf: and they said, This is thygod, Israel, who has brought thee up out of the land of Egypt!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Aaron saw [it], and built an altar before it; and Aaron made a proclamation, and said, To-morrow is a feast to Jehovah!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they rose up early on the morrow, and offered up burnt-offerings, and brought peace-offerings; and the people sat down to eat and to drink, and rose up to sport.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then Jehovah said to Moses, Away, go down! for thy people, which thou hast brought out of the land of Egypt, is acting corruptly.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They have turned aside quickly out of the way that I commanded them: they have made themselves a molten calf, and have bowed down to it, and have sacrificed thereunto, and said, This is thygod, Israel, who has brought thee up out of the land of Egypt!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah said to Moses, I see this people, and behold, it is a stiff-necked people.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And now let me alone, that my anger may burn against them, and I may consume them; and I will make of thee a great nation.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses besought Jehovah hisGod, and said, Why, Jehovah, doth thy wrath burn against thy people, which thou hast brought forth out of the land of Egypt with great power and with a strong hand?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Why should the Egyptians speak, and say, For misfortune he has brought them out, to slay them on the mountains, and to annihilate them from the face of the earth? Turn from the heat of thine anger, and repent of this evil against thy people!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Remember Abraham, Isaac and Israel, thy servants, to whom thou sworest by thyself, and saidst to them, I will multiply your seed as the stars of heaven, and all this land that I have spoken of will I give to your seed, and they shall possess [it] for ever!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah repented of the evil that he had said he would do to his people.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses turned and went down from the mountain, [with] the two tables of the testimony in his hand — tables written on both their sides: on this side and on that were they written.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the tables [were]God's work, and the writing wasGod's writing, engraven on the tables.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Joshua heard the noise of the people as they shouted, and said to Moses, There is a shout of war in the camp.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said, It is not the sound of a shout of victory, neither is it the sound of a shout of defeat: it is the noise of alternate singing I hear.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass, when he came near the camp, and saw the calf and the dancing, that Moses' anger burned, and he cast the tables out of his hands, and shattered them beneath the mountain.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he took the calf that they had made, and burned [it] with fire, and ground it to powder, and strewed [it] on the water, and made the children of Israel drink [it].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses said to Aaron, What has this people done to thee, that thou hast brought so great a sin on them?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Aaron said, Let not the anger of my lord burn! thou knowest the people, that they are [set] on mischief.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they said to me, Make us agod, who will go before us; for this Moses, the man that has brought us up out of the land of Egypt, we do not know what is become of him!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I said to them, Who has gold? They broke [it] off, and gave [it] me, and I cast it into the fire, and there came out this calf.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses saw the people how they were stripped; for Aaron had stripped them to [their] shame before their adversaries.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses stood in the gate of the camp, and said, He that is for Jehovah, [let him come] to me. And all the sons of Levi gathered to him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to them, Thus saith Jehovah, theGod of Israel: Put every man his sword upon his hip; go and return from gate to gate through the camp, and slay every man his brother, and every man his friend, and every man his neighbour.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the sons of Levi did according to the word of Moses; and there fell of the people that day about three thousand men.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses said, Consecrate yourselves to-day to Jehovah, yea, every man with his son, and with his brother, and bring on yourselves a blessing to-day.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass the next day, that Moses said to the people, Ye have sinned a great sin. And now I will go up to Jehovah: perhaps I shall make atonement for your sin.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses returned to Jehovah, and said, Alas, this people has sinned a great sin, and they have made themselves agod of gold!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And now, if thou wilt forgive their sin...but if not, blot me, I pray thee, out of thy book that thou hast written.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah said to Moses, Whoever hath sinned against me, him will I blot out of my book.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And now go, lead the people whither I have told thee: behold, my Angel shall go before thee; but in the day of my visiting I will visit their sin upon them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah smote the people, because they made the calf, which Aaron had made.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah said to Moses, Depart, go up hence, thou and the people that thou hast brought up out of the land of Egypt, into the land that I swore unto Abraham, to Isaac, and to Jacob, saying, Unto thy seed will I give it,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: (and I will send an angel before thee, and dispossess the Canaanite, the Amorite, and the Hittite, and the Perizzite, the Hivite, and the Jebusite,)

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: into a land flowing with milk and honey; for I will not go up in the midst of thee, for thou art a stiff-necked people, — lest I consume thee on the way.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when the people heard this evil word, they mourned; and no man put on his ornaments.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now Jehovah had said to Moses, Say unto the children of Israel, Ye are a stiff-necked people: in one moment I will come up into the midst of thee and will consume thee. And now put off thine ornaments from thee, and I will know what I will do unto thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the children of Israel stripped themselves of their ornaments at mount Horeb.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses took the tent, and pitched it outside the camp, far from the camp, and called it the Tent of meeting. And it came to pass [that] every one who sought Jehovah went out to the tent of meeting which was outside the camp.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass, when Moses went out to the tent, all the people rose up, and stood every man at the entrance of his tent, and they looked after Moses until he entered into the tent.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass when Moses entered into the tent, the pillar of cloud descended, and stood at the entrance of the tent, and [Jehovah] talked with Moses.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And all the people saw the pillar of cloud standing at the entrance of the tent; and all the people rose and worshipped, every man at the entrance of his tent.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah spoke with Moses face to face, as a man speaks with his friend. And he returned to the camp; but his attendant, Joshua the son of Nun, a young man, departed not from within the tent.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses said to Jehovah, Behold, thou sayest unto me, Bring up this people; but thou dost not let me know whom thou wilt send with me; and thou hast said, I know thee by name, and thou hast also found grace in mine eyes.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And now, if indeed I have found grace in thine eyes, make me now to know thy way, that I may know thee, that I may find grace in thine eyes; and consider that this nation is thy people!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said, My presence shall go, and I will give thee rest.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to him, If thy presence do not go, bring us not up hence.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And how shall it be known then that I have found grace in thine eyes — I and thy people? [Is it] not by thy going with us? so shall we be distinguished, I and thy people, from every people that is on the face of the earth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah said to Moses, I will do this thing also that thou hast said; for thou hast found grace in mine eyes, and I know thee by name.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said, Let me, I pray thee, see thy glory.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said, I will make all my goodness pass before thy face, and I will proclaim the name of Jehovah before thee; and I will be gracious to whom I will be gracious, and I will shew mercy on whom I will shew mercy.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said, Thou canst not see my face; for Man shall not see me, and live.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah said, Behold, [there is] a place by me: there shalt thou stand on the rock.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it shall come to pass, when my glory passeth by, that I will put thee in a cleft of the rock, and will cover thee with my hand, until I have passed by.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I will take away my hand, and thou shalt see me from behind; but my face shall not be seen.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah said to Moses, Hew for thyself two tables of stone like the first; and I will write upon the tables the words that were upon the first tables, which thou hast broken.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And be ready for the morning, and go up in the morning to mount Sinai, and stand there before me on the top of the mountain.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And let no man go up with thee, neither shall any man be seen on all the mountain; neither shall sheep and oxen feed in front of that mountain.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he hewed two tables of stone like the first; and Moses rose up early in the morning and went up to mount Sinai, as Jehovah had commanded him, and took in his hand the two tables of stone.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah came down in the cloud, and stood beside him there, and proclaimed the name of Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah passed by before his face, and proclaimed, Jehovah, JehovahGod merciful and gracious, slow to anger, and abundant in goodness and truth,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: keeping mercy unto thousands, forgiving iniquity and transgression and sin, but by no means clearing [the guilty]; visiting the iniquity of the fathers upon the children, and upon the children's children, upon the third and upon the fourth [generation].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses made haste, and bowed his head to the earth and worshipped,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and said, If indeed I have found grace in thine eyes, Lord, let the Lord, I pray thee, go in our midst; for it is a stiff-necked people; and pardon our iniquity and our sin, and take us for an inheritance!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said, Behold, I make a covenant: before all thy people I will do marvels that have not been done in all the earth, nor in any nation; and all the people in the midst of which thou [art] shall see the work of Jehovah; for a terrible thing it shall be that I will do with thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Observe what I command thee this day: behold, I will drive out before thee the Amorite, and the Canaanite, and the Hittite, and the Perizzite, and the Hivite, and the Jebusite.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Take heed to thyself, that thou make no covenant with the inhabitants of the land to which thou shalt come, lest it be a snare in the midst of thee;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but ye shall demolish their altars, shatter their statues, and hew down their Asherahs.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For thou shalt worship no otherGod; for Jehovah — Jealous is his name — is a jealousGod;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: lest thou make a covenant with the inhabitants of the land, and then, when they go a whoring after theirgods, and sacrifice unto theirgods, thou be invited, and eat of their sacrifice,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and thou take of their daughters unto thy sons, and their daughters go a whoring after theirgods, and make thy sons go a whoring after theirgods.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: — Thou shalt make thyself no moltengods.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: — The feast of the unleavened bread shalt thou keep: seven days shalt thou eat unleavened bread, as I have commanded thee, at the appointed time of the month Abib; for in the month Abib thou camest out from Egypt.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: — All that openeth the womb [is] mine; and all the cattle that is born a male, the firstling of ox and sheep.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But the firstling of an ass thou shalt ransom with a lamb; and if thou ransom [it] not, then shalt thou break its neck. All the first-born of thy sons thou shalt ransom; and none shall appear before me empty.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: — Six days shalt thou work, but on the seventh day thou shalt rest; in ploughing time and in harvest thou shalt rest.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: — And thou shalt observe the feast of weeks, of the first-fruits of wheat-harvest, and the feast of ingathering at the turn of the year.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thrice in the year shall all thy males appear before the Lord Jehovah, theGod of Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For I will dispossess the nations before thee, and enlarge thy border, and no man shall desire thy land, when thou goest up to appear before the face of Jehovah thyGod thrice in the year.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: — Thou shalt not offer the blood of my sacrifice with leaven; neither shall the sacrifice of the feast of the passover be left over night until the morning.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: — The first of the first-fruits of thy land shalt thou bring into the house of Jehovah thyGod. Thou shalt not boil a kid in its mother's milk.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah said to Moses, Write thee these words; for after the tenor of these words have I made a covenant with thee and with Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: — And he was there with Jehovah forty days and forty nights; he ate no bread, and drank no water. — And he wrote on the tables the words of the covenant, the ten words.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass, when Moses came down from mount Sinai — and the two tables of testimony were in Moses' hand, when he came down from the mountain — that Moses knew not that the skin of his face shone through his talking with him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Aaron and all the children of Israel saw Moses, and behold, the skin of his face shone; and they were afraid to come near him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses called to them; and they turned to him, — Aaron and all the principal men of the assembly; and Moses talked with them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And afterwards, all the children of Israel came near; and he gave them in commandment all that Jehovah had spoken with him on mount Sinai.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses ended speaking with them; and he had put on his face a veil.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when Moses went in before Jehovah to speak with him, he took the veil off, until he came out; and he came out, and spoke to the children of Israel what he was commanded.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the children of Israel saw the face of Moses, that the skin of Moses' face shone; and Moses put the veil on his face again, until he went in to speak with him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses collected all the assembly of the children of Israel, and said to them, These are the things which Jehovah has commanded, to do them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Six days shall work be done, but on the seventh day there shall be to you a holy day, a sabbath of rest to Jehovah: whoever does work on it shall be put to death.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Ye shall kindle no fire throughout your dwellings upon the sabbath day.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses spoke to all the assembly of the children of Israel, saying, This is the word which Jehovah has commanded, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Take from among you a heave-offering to Jehovah: every one whose heart [is] willing, let him bring it, Jehovah's heave-offering — gold, and silver, and copper,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and blue, and purple, and scarlet, and byssus, and goats' [hair],

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and rams' skins dyed red, and badgers' skins, and acacia-wood,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and oil for the light, and spices for the anointing oil, and for the incense of fragrant drugs;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and onyx stones, and stones to be set for the ephod, and for the breastplate.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And all who are wise-hearted among you shall come and make all that Jehovah has commanded:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: the tabernacle, its tent, and its covering, its clasps, and its boards, its bars, its pillars, and its bases;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: the ark, and its staves; the mercy-seat, and the veil of separation;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: the table and its staves, and all its utensils, and the shewbread;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and the lamp-stand for the light, and its utensils, and its lamps, and the oil for the light;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and the altar of incense, and its staves; and the anointing-oil, and the incense of fragrant drugs; and the entrance-curtain at the entrance of the tabernacle;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: the altar of burnt-offering, and the copper grating for it, its staves, and all its utensils; the laver and its stand;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: the hangings of the court, its pillars, and its bases, and the curtains of the gate of the court;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: the pegs of the tabernacle, and the pegs of the court, and their cords;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: the garments of service, to do service in the sanctuary, the holy garments for Aaron the priest, and the garments of his sons, to serve as priests.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And all the assembly of the children of Israel departed from before Moses.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they came, every one whose heart moved him, and every one whose spirit prompted him; they brought Jehovah's heave-offering for the work of the tent of meeting, and for all its service, and for the holy garments.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they came, both men and women; every one who was of willing heart brought nose-rings, and earrings, and rings, and bracelets, all kinds of utensils of gold: every man that waved a wave-offering of gold to Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And every man with whom was found blue, and purple, and scarlet, and byssus, and goats' [hair], and rams' skins dyed red, and badgers' skins, brought [them].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: All they that offered a heave-offering of silver and copper brought Jehovah's heave-offering. And every one with whom was found acacia-wood for all manner of work of the service, brought [it].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And every woman that was wise-hearted spun with her hands, and brought what she had spun: the blue, and the purple, and the scarlet, and the byssus.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And all the women whose heart moved them in wisdom spun goats' [hair].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the principal men brought the onyx stones, and the stones to be set, for the ephod, and for the breastplate;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and the spice, and the oil for the light, and for the anointing oil, and for the incense of fragrant drugs.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The children of Israel brought a voluntary offering to Jehovah, every man and woman whose heart prompted them to bring for all manner of work, which Jehovah, by the hand of Moses, had commanded to be done.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses said to the children of Israel, See, Jehovah has called by name Bezaleel the son of Uri, the son of Hur, of the tribe of Judah,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and he has filled him with the spirit ofGod, in wisdom, in understanding, and in knowledge, and in all manner of workmanship,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and to devise artistic things: to work in gold, and in silver, and in copper,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and in cutting of stones, for setting, and in carving of wood, to execute all artistic work;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and he has put in his heart to teach, he and Aholiab, the son of Ahisamach, of the tribe of Dan:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: he has filled them with wisdom of heart, to work all manner of work of the engraver, and of the artificer, and of the embroiderer, in blue, and in purple, in scarlet, and in byssus, and of the weaver, [even] of them that do every kind of work, and of those that devise artistic work.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then wrought Bezaleel and Aholiab, and every man that was wise-hearted, in whom Jehovah had put wisdom and understanding to know how to work all manner of work of the service of the sanctuary — according to all that Jehovah had commanded.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses called Bezaleel and Aholiab, and every man that was wise-hearted, in whose heartGod had put wisdom, every one whose heart moved him to come to the work to do it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they took from Moses every heave-offering that the children of Israel had brought for the work of the service of the sanctuary, to make it. And they still brought him voluntary offerings morning by morning.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And all the wise men that wrought all the work of the sanctuary came, every man from his work which they wrought,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and spoke to Moses, saying, The people bring much more than enough for the service of the work that Jehovah commanded to be done.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then Moses gave commandment, and they caused it to be proclaimed through the camp, saying, Let neither man nor woman make any more work for the heave-offering of the sanctuary! So the people were restrained from bringing;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for the work they had was sufficient for all the work to do it, and it was too much.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And every wise-hearted man among those that wrought the work of the tabernacle made ten curtains of twined byssus, and blue, and purple, and scarlet: [with] cherubim of artistic work did he make them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The length of one curtain was twenty-eight cubits, and the breadth of one curtain four cubits — one measure for all the curtains.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he coupled five of the curtains one to another, and [the other] five curtains coupled he one to another.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he made loops of blue on the edge of one curtain at the edge of the coupling; he did likewise in the edge of the outermost curtain in the other coupling.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He made fifty loops in one curtain, and he made fifty loops at the end of the curtain that was in the other coupling: the loops were opposite to one another.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he made fifty clasps of gold, and coupled the curtains together with the clasps, so that the tabernacle became one.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he made curtains of goats' [hair] for the tent over the tabernacle: eleven curtains did he make them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The length of one curtain was thirty cubits, and four cubits the breadth of one curtain — one measure for the eleven curtains.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he coupled five of the curtains by themselves, and six of the curtains by themselves.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he made fifty loops on the edge of the outermost curtain in the coupling, and fifty loops made he on the edge of the curtain in the other coupling.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he made fifty clasps of copper to couple the tent, that it might be one.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he made a covering for the tent [of] rams' skins dyed red, and a covering of badgers' skins above [that].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he made the boards for the tabernacle of acacia-wood, standing up;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: ten cubits the length of the boards, and one cubit and a half the breadth of one board;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: two tenons in one board, connected one with the other: thus did he make for all the boards of the tabernacle.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he made the boards for the tabernacle: twenty boards for the south side southward;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and he made forty bases of silver under the twenty boards, two bases under one board, for its two tenons, and two bases under another board for its two tenons.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And for the other side of the tabernacle, on the side toward the north, he made twenty boards,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and their forty bases of silver, two bases under one board, and two bases under another board.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And at the rear of the tabernacle, westward, he made six boards;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and he made two boards for the corners of the tabernacle at the rear;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and they were joined beneath, and were coupled together at the top thereof into one ring: thus he did to both of them in both the corners;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and there were eight boards, and their silver bases: sixteen bases, under every board two bases.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: — And he made bars of acacia-wood: five for the boards of the one side of the tabernacle,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and five bars for the boards of the other side of the tabernacle, and five bars for the boards of the tabernacle at the rear, westward.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he made the middle bar in the midst of the boards reach from one end to the other.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he overlaid the boards with gold; and made their rings of gold [as] receptacles for the bars; and overlaid the bars with gold.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he made the veil of blue, and purple, and scarlet, and twined byssus: of artistic work he made it [with] cherubim.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he made four pillars of acacia[-wood] for it, and overlaid them with gold; their hooks were of gold; and he cast for them four bases of silver.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he made a curtain for the entrance of the tent of blue, and purple, and scarlet, and twined byssus, of embroidery;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and its five pillars with their hooks; and he overlaid their capitals and their connecting-rods with gold; and their five bases were of copper.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Bezaleel made the ark of acacia-wood; two cubits and a half the length thereof, and a cubit and a half the breadth thereof, and a cubit and a half the height thereof.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he overlaid it with pure gold inside and outside, and made a border of gold upon it round about.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he cast for it four rings of gold, for its four corners: two rings on the one side of it, and two rings on the other side of it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he made staves of acacia-wood and overlaid them with gold.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he put the staves into the rings on the sides of the ark, to carry the ark.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: — And he made a mercy-seat of pure gold; two cubits and a half the length thereof, and one cubit and a half the breadth thereof.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he made two cherubim of gold; of beaten work did he make them, at the two ends of the mercy-seat;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: one cherub at the end of one side, and one cherub at the end of the other side; out of the mercy-seat he made the two cherubim at the two ends thereof.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the cherubim spread out [their] wings over it, covering over with their wings the mercy-seat; and their faces were opposite to one another: the faces of the cherubim were [turned] toward the mercy-seat.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he made the table of acacia-wood; two cubits the length thereof, and a cubit the breadth thereof, and a cubit and a half the height thereof.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he overlaid it with pure gold, and made upon it a border of gold round about.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he made for it a margin of a hand-breadth round about; and made a border of gold for the margin thereof round about.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he cast for it four rings of gold, and put the rings on the four corners that were on the four feet thereof.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Close to the margin were the rings, as receptacles of the staves to carry the table.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he made the staves of acacia-wood, and overlaid them with gold, to carry the table.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he made the utensils that were on the table, the dishes thereof, and the cups thereof, and the bowls thereof, and the goblets with which to pour out, of pure gold.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he made the candlestick of pure gold; [of] beaten work he made the candlestick: its base, and its shaft, its cups, its knobs, and its flowers were of itself.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And six branches went out of the sides thereof — three branches of the candlestick out of the one side thereof, and three branches of the candlestick out of the other side thereof;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: [there were] three cups shaped like almonds in the one branch, a knob and a flower; and three cups shaped like almonds in the other branch, a knob and a flower: so in the six branches which went out of the candlestick.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And in the candlestick were four cups, shaped like almonds, its knobs, and its flowers;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and a knob under two branches thereof, and [again] a knob under two branches thereof, and [again] a knob under two branches thereof, for the six branches which went out of it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Their knobs and their branches were of itself — all of one beaten work of pure gold.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he made the seven lamps thereof, and the snuffers thereof, and the snuff-trays thereof, of pure gold.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Of a talent of pure gold he made it, and all its utensils.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he made the altar of incense of acacia-wood; a cubit the length thereof, and a cubit the breadth thereof, square, and two cubits the height thereof: its horns were of itself.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he overlaid it with pure gold, the top thereof, and the sides thereof round about, and the horns thereof; and made upon it a border of gold round about.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he made two rings of gold for it under its border, by its two corners, on the two sides thereof, as receptacles for the staves with which to carry it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he made the staves of acacia-wood, and overlaid them with gold.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he made the holy anointing oil, and the pure incense of fragrant drugs, according to the work of the perfumer.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he made the altar of burnt-offering of acacia-wood; five cubits the length thereof, and five cubits the breadth thereof, square, and three cubits the height thereof.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he made its horns on the four corners thereof; its horns were of itself; and he overlaid it with copper.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he made all the utensils of the altar: the pots, and the shovels, and the bowls, the forks, and the firepans; all its utensils made he of copper.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he made for the altar a grating of network of copper under its ledge from beneath, to the very middle of it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he cast four rings for the four corners of the grating of copper, as receptacles for the staves.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he made the staves of acacia-wood, and overlaid them with copper.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he put the staves into the rings on the sides of the altar, with which to carry it. Hollow with boards did he make it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he made the laver of copper, and its stand of copper, of the mirrors of the crowds of women who crowded before the entrance of the tent of meeting.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he made the court. On the south side southward, the hangings of the court were of twined byssus, a hundred cubits;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: their pillars twenty, and their bases twenty, of copper; the hooks of the pillars and their connecting-rods of silver.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And on the north side, a hundred cubits; their pillars twenty, and their bases twenty, of copper; the hooks of the pillars and their connecting-rods of silver.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And on the west side, hangings of fifty cubits; their pillars ten, and their bases ten; the hooks of the pillars and their connecting-rods of silver.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And for the east side, eastward, fifty cubits;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: the hangings on the one wing of fifteen cubits, their pillars three, and their bases three;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and on the other wing, on this side as on that side of the gate of the court, hangings of fifteen cubits, their pillars three, and their bases three.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: All the hangings of the court round about were of twined byssus;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and the bases of the pillars of copper, the hooks of the pillars and their connecting-rods of silver, and the overlaying of their capitals of silver; and all the pillars of the court were fastened together with [rods of] silver.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: — And the curtain of the gate of the court was of embroidery of blue, and purple, and scarlet, and twined byssus; and the length was twenty cubits, and the height like the breadth, five cubits, just as the hangings of the court;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and their pillars four, and their bases four, of copper; their hooks of silver, and the overlaying of their capitals and their connecting-rods of silver.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And all the pegs for the tabernacle and for the court round about were of copper.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: These are the things numbered of the tabernacle, the tabernacle of the testimony, which were counted, according to the commandment of Moses, by the service of the Levites, under the hand of Ithamar, son of Aaron the priest.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Bezaleel the son of Uri, the son of Hur, of the tribe of Judah, made all that Jehovah had commanded Moses;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and with him Aholiab, son of Ahisamach, of the tribe of Dan, an engraver and artificer, and an embroiderer in blue, and in purple, and in scarlet, and in byssus.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: All the gold that it took for the work in all the work of the sanctuary — the gold of the wave-offering, was twenty-nine talents, and seven hundred and thirty shekels, according to the shekel of the sanctuary.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the silver of them that were numbered of the assembly was a hundred talents, and a thousand seven hundred and seventy-five shekels, according to the shekel of the sanctuary:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: a bekah the head — half a shekel, according to the shekel of the sanctuary, for every one that passed the numbering from twenty years old and upward, [of] the six hundred and three thousand five hundred and fifty.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And there were a hundred talents of silver for casting the bases of the sanctuary, and the bases of the veil; a hundred bases of a hundred talents, a talent for a base.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And of the thousand seven hundred and seventy-five [shekels] he made the hooks for the pillars, and overlaid their capitals, and fastened them [with rods].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the copper of the wave-offering was seventy talents, and two thousand four hundred shekels.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he made with it the bases for the entrance of the tent of meeting, and the copper altar, and the copper grating for it, and all the utensils of the altar.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the bases of the court round about, and the bases of the gate of the court, and all the pegs of the tabernacle, and all the pegs of the court round about.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And of the blue and purple and scarlet they made garments of service, for service in the sanctuary, and made the holy garments for Aaron; as Jehovah had commanded Moses.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he made the ephod of gold, blue, and purple, and scarlet, and twined byssus.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they beat the gold into thin plates, and cut it [into] wires, to work it artistically into the blue, and into the purple, and into the scarlet, and into the byssus.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They made shoulder-pieces for it, joining it: at its two ends was it joined together.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the girdle of his ephod, which was on it, was of the same, according to its work, of gold, blue, and purple, and scarlet, and twined byssus; as Jehovah had commanded Moses.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they wrought the onyx stones mounted in enclosures of gold, engraved with the engravings of a seal, according to the names of the sons of Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he put them on the shoulder-pieces of the ephod, [as] stones of memorial for the children of Israel; as Jehovah had commanded Moses.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he made the breastplate of artistic work, like the work of the ephod, of gold, blue, and purple, and scarlet, and twined byssus.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: It was square; double did they make the breastplate, a span the length thereof, and a span the breadth thereof, doubled.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they set in it four rows of stones: [one] row, a sardoin, a topaz, and an emerald — the first row;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and the second row, a carbuncle, a sapphire, and a diamond;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and the third row, an opal, an agate, and an amethyst;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and the fourth row, a chrysolite, an onyx, and a jasper; mounted in enclosures of gold in their settings.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the stones were according to the names of the children of Israel, twelve, according to their names, engraved as a seal, every one according to his name, for the twelve tribes.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they made on the breastplate chains of laced work of wreathen work, of pure gold.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they made two settings of gold, and two gold rings, and put the two rings on the two ends of the breastplate.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they put the two wreathen [cords] of gold in the two rings on the ends of the breastplate;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and the two ends of the two wreathen [cords] they fastened to the two settings, and put them on the shoulder-pieces of the ephod, on the front thereof.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they made two rings of gold, and put [them] on the two ends of the breastplate, on the border thereof, which faceth the ephod inwards.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they made two rings of gold, and put them upon the two shoulder-pieces of the ephod underneath, to the front thereof, just by the coupling thereof, above the girdle of the ephod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they bound the breastplate with its rings to the rings of the ephod with lace of blue, that it might be above the girdle of the ephod, and that the breastplate might not be loosed from the ephod; as Jehovah had commanded Moses.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he made the cloak of the ephod of woven work, all of blue;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and the opening of the cloak in its middle, as the opening of a coat of mail; a binding was round about the opening, that it should not rend.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they made on the skirts of the cloak pomegranates of blue and purple and scarlet, twined.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they made bells of pure gold, and put the bells between the pomegranates, in the skirts of the cloak, round about, between the pomegranates:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: a bell and a pomegranate, a bell and a pomegranate, in the skirts of the cloak round about, for service; as Jehovah had commanded Moses.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they made the vests of byssus of woven work, for Aaron, and for his sons;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and the turban of byssus; and the ornamental caps, of byssus; and the linen trousers, of twined byssus;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and the girdle, of twined byssus, and blue, and purple, and scarlet, of embroidery; as Jehovah had commanded Moses.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they made the thin plate, the holy diadem, of pure gold, and wrote on it with the writing of the engravings of a seal, Holiness to Jehovah!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they fastened to it a lace of blue, to fasten [it] on the turban above; as Jehovah had commanded Moses.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And all the labour of the tabernacle of the tent of meeting was ended; and the children of Israel had done [it] according to all that Jehovah had commanded Moses — so had they done [it].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they brought the tabernacle to Moses — the tent, and all its utensils, its clasps, its boards, its bars, and its pillars, and its bases;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and the covering of rams' skins dyed red, and the covering of badgers' skins, and the veil of separation;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: the ark of the testimony, and its staves, and the mercy-seat;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: the table, all its utensils, and the shewbread;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: the pure candlestick, its lamps, the lamps set in order, and all its utensils, and the oil for the light;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and the golden altar, and the anointing oil, and the incense of fragrant drugs; and the curtain for the entrance of the tent;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: the copper altar, and the copper grating that was for it, its staves and all its utensils; the laver and its stand;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: the hangings of the court, its pillars, and its bases; and the curtain for the gate of the court, its cords, and its pegs; and all the vessel's of service of the tabernacle, for the tent of meeting;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: the garments of service, for service in the sanctuary; the holy garments for Aaron the priest, and his sons' garments, to serve as priests.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: According to all that Jehovah had commanded Moses, so had the children of Israel done all the labour.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses saw all the work, and behold, they had done it as Jehovah had commanded — so had they done [it]; and Moses blessed them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah spoke to Moses, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: On the day of the first month, on the first of the month, shalt thou set up the tabernacle of the tent of meeting.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt put in it the ark of the testimony, and shalt cover the ark with the veil.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt bring in the table, and set in order the things that are to be set in order upon it. And thou shalt bring in the candlestick and light its lamps.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt set the golden altar for the incense before the ark of the testimony; and hang up the curtain of the entrance to the tabernacle.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt set the altar of burnt-offering before the entrance of the tabernacle of the tent of meeting.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt set the laver between the tent of meeting and the altar, and shalt put water in it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt fix the court round about, and hang up the curtain at the gate of the court.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt take the anointing oil, and anoint the tabernacle, and all that is in it, and shalt hallow it, and all its utensils; and it shall be holy.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt anoint the altar of burnt-offering, and all its utensils; and thou shalt hallow the altar, and the altar shall be most holy.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt anoint the laver and its stand, and hallow it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt bring Aaron and his sons near, at the entrance of the tent of meeting, and bathe them with water.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt clothe Aaron with the holy garments, and anoint him, and hallow him, that he may serve me as priest.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt bring his sons near, and clothe them with vests.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt anoint them, as thou didst anoint their father, that they may serve me as priests. And their anointing shall be to them an everlasting priesthood throughout their generations.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses did so: as Jehovah had commanded him, so did he.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass in the first month in the second year, on the first of the month, that the tabernacle was set up.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses set up the tabernacle, and put in its bases, and fixed its boards, and put in its bars, and set up its pillars.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he spread the tent over the tabernacle, and put the covering of the tent above over it; as Jehovah had commanded Moses.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he took and put the testimony into the ark, and put the staves in the ark, and put the mercy-seat above on the ark.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he brought the ark into the tabernacle, and hung up the veil of separation, and covered the ark of the testimony; as Jehovah had commanded Moses.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he put the table in the tent of meeting, on the side of the tabernacle northward, outside the veil,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and arranged the bread in order upon it before Jehovah; as Jehovah had commanded Moses.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he put the candlestick in the tent of meeting, opposite to the table, on the side of the tabernacle southward.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he lighted the lamps before Jehovah; as Jehovah had commanded Moses.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he put the golden altar in the tent of meeting before the veil.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he burnt on it fragrant incense; as Jehovah had commanded Moses.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he hung up the curtain of the entrance to the tabernacle.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he put the altar of burnt-offering at the entrance to the tabernacle of the tent of meeting, and offered on it the burnt-offering and the oblation; as Jehovah had commanded Moses.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he set the laver between the tent of meeting and the altar, and put water in it for washing.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses and Aaron and his sons washed their hands and their feet out of it:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: when they went into the tent of meeting, and when they drew near to the altar, they washed; as Jehovah had commanded Moses.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he set up the court round about the tabernacle and the altar, and hung up the curtain of the gate of the court. And so Moses finished the work.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the cloud covered the tent of meeting, and the glory of Jehovah filled the tabernacle.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses could not enter into the tent of meeting, for the cloud abode on it, and the glory of Jehovah filled the tabernacle.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when the cloud was taken up from over the tabernacle, the children of Israel journeyed in all their journeys.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And if the cloud were not taken up, then they did not journey until the day that it was taken up.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For the cloud of Jehovah was on the tabernacle by day, and fire was in it by night, before the eyes of all the house of Israel, throughout all their journeys.
